Syntax: show evpn evi <EVI-ID> mld snooping
For 4100i AOS-CX 10.17.1000, what does the show evpn vtep-neighbor command do?

The show evpn vtep-neighbor command shows the remote VTEPs MAC-IP binding. The state of the peer VTEP denotes whether VXLAN tunnel to the VTEP is Up or Down. Public show evpn vtep-neighbor 202 Display information for all VRFs. Specify a VRF by VRF name (if no <VRF-NAME> is specifie d, the default VRF is implied. Syntax: show evpn vtep-neighbor {all-vrfs | vrf <VRF-Name>}.
For 4100i AOS-CX 10.17.1000, what is the syntax of the show evpn vtep-neighbor command?
Syntax: show evpn vtep-neighbor {all-vrfs | vrf <VRF-Name>}
For 4100i AOS-CX 10.17.1000, what does the clear dsm statistics command do?

The clear dsm statistics command clears the statistics of the interfaces attached to the distributed services module. Shows the slot ID of the DSM. <SLOT-ID> global Clears the DSM interface statistics for all sessions. Public DSM commands 757. Syntax: clear dsm <SLOT-ID> statistics [global].
For 4100i AOS-CX 10.17.1000, what is the syntax of the clear dsm statistics command?
Syntax: clear dsm <SLOT-ID> statistics [global]
For 4100i AOS-CX 10.17.1000, what does the diag dsm console command do?

The diag dsm console command provides access to the operator console of the attached distributed services module. From this console, operator commands can be run. Exiting the console returns the user back to CLI. This is intended for diagnostic purposes only. Public diag dsm console 758 Shows the slot ID of the DSM. <SLOT-ID>. Syntax: diag dsm console <SLOT-ID>.
For 4100i AOS-CX 10.17.1000, what is the syntax of the diag dsm console command?
Syntax: diag dsm console <SLOT-ID>
For 4100i AOS-CX 10.17.1000, what is the syntax of the show dsm command?
Syntax: show dsm <SLOT-ID>
For 4100i AOS-CX 10.17.1000, what does the show dsm erspan command do?
The show dsm erspan command displays the status of the Encapsulated Remote Switched Port Analyzer (ERSPAN) feature for DSM. Public show dsm erspan 765. Syntax: show dsm erspan.
For 4100i AOS-CX 10.17.1000, what is the syntax of the show dsm erspan command?
Syntax: show dsm erspan
For 4100i AOS-CX 10.17.1000, what does the interface vxlan command do?

The interface vxlan command creates VXLAN interface 1 and changes to the config-vxlan-if context. A maximum of one VXLAN interface is supported. By default, the VXLAN is disabled. To enable the VXLAN, use the command no shutdown. The no form of this command removes VXLAN interface 1. This deletes the VXLAN tunnel, and all VNIs and VLAN-to-VNI mappings associated with it. Only one VXLAN interface is supported. (Optional) Specify if the interface tunnel uses IPv4 or IPv6 add ressing. If this parameter is not included, the interface default is IPv6. Changes the inter-VXLAN bridging mode on tunnels where VXLAN bridging across tunnels is disabled by default. Default mode is deny. The no form of this command sets the inter-VXLAN bridging mode to the default mode of deny. Syntax: interface vxlan 1 [mode IPv4|IPv6].
For 4100i AOS-CX 10.17.1000, what is the syntax of the interface vxlan command?
Syntax: interface vxlan 1 [mode IPv4|IPv6]
For 4100i AOS-CX 10.17.1000, what does the ip-fragment-bypass command do?

The ip-fragment-bypass command this command toggles IP fragment bypass functionality for a VLAN. When enabled, IP fragment packets that are normally redirected to the Distributed Services Module, are not redirected. This is configured on a per-VLAN basis. Syntax: ip-fragment-bypass.
For 4100i AOS-CX 10.17.1000, what is the syntax of the ip-fragment-bypass command?
Syntax: ip-fragment-bypass
For 4100i AOS-CX 10.17.1000, what is the syntax of the show interface vxlan command?
Syntax: show interface vxlan <IFNAME>
For 4100i AOS-CX 10.17.1000, what does the show interface vxlan vni command do?

The show interface vxlan vni command shows detailed VNI information for the VXLAN interface. Specifies the number for a given VNI. Range: 1 to 16777214. Shows the VTEPS information for a specified VNI. transport-VRFshows the transport VRFstatus for a specified VNI. Syntax: show interface vxlan vni <VNI-ID> [vteps | transport-vrf].
For 4100i AOS-CX 10.17.1000, what is the syntax of the show interface vxlan vni command?
Syntax: show interface vxlan vni <VNI-ID> [vteps | transport-vrf]
For 4100i AOS-CX 10.17.1000, what does the show interface vxlan vteps command do?

The show interface vxlan vteps command displays detailed VTEP information for the VXLAN interface. VTEP states can be one of the following: • operational: Tunnel endpoint is fully programmed on the switch hardware. • configuration_error: Tunnel endpoint programming in the switch hardware failed due to misconfiguration. • no_hw_resources: Tunnel endpoint programming failed in the switch hardware due to insufficient resources. • activating: Initial state of tunnel endpoint when it is configured. When a tunnel endpoint is a directly connected via nexthop, then nexthop reachability appears empty (--). Show detail VTEP information. Specifies the IP address of a VTEP peer in IPv4 format (x.x.x.x), where x is a decimal number from 0 to 255. Specifies the IP address of a VTEP peer in IPv6 format (X: X: : X: X). Syntax: show interface vxlan vteps [detail | <IP-ADDR>].
For 4100i AOS-CX 10.17.1000, what is the syntax of the show interface vxlan vteps command?
Syntax: show interface vxlan vteps [detail | <IP-ADDR>]
For 4100i AOS-CX 10.17.1000, what does the show vlan ip-fragment-bypass command do?

The show vlan ip-fragment-bypass command this command displays the status of VLANs with IP fragment bypass enabled. Disables the VXLAN interface and deletes all VXLAN tunnels, segments, and members on the interface. Public shutdown 816 The no form of this command starts the VXLAN interface and creates all VXLAN tunnels and segments. If members are configured, they are added to the VXLAN segment. Configures the source IPv4 address for a VXLAN interface. All VXLAN encapsulated packets use this source IP address in the outer IP header. If you change an existing source IP address, all tunnels with the old source IP address are deleted, and new tunnels are created with the new source IP address. Syntax: show vlan ip-fragment-bypass.
For 4100i AOS-CX 10.17.1000, what is the syntax of the show vlan ip-fragment-bypass command?
Syntax: show vlan ip-fragment-bypass

section: Replacing a VSF Stack Member Using a Cloud-Managed Feature Pack

messages.content: For 4100i AOS-CX 10.17.1000, how do you replacing a VSF Stack Member Using a Cloud-Managed Feature Pack?

To replacing a VSF Stack Member Using a Cloud-Managed Feature Pack, follow the documented procedure: 1. Log on to the command-line interface of the VSF stack member 2. Verify that the advanced feature pack is being consumed by all switch members.

task: Replacing a VSF Stack Member Using a Cloud-Managed Feature Pack

section: Replacing a VSF Stack Member Using a File-Based Feature Pack

messages.content: For 4100i AOS-CX 10.17.1000, how do you replacing a VSF Stack Member Using a File-Based Feature Pack?

To replacing a VSF Stack Member Using a File-Based Feature Pack, follow the documented procedure: 1. Log on to the switch command-line interface 2. Use the no vsf member <member> command to remove the member you want to replace from the VSF 3. After you remove the device from the VSF stack, features supported by the feature packs are still active.

task: Replacing a VSF Stack Member Using a File-Based Feature Pack

section: Replacing a VSF Stack Conductor Using a File-Based Feature Pack

messages.content: For 4100i AOS-CX 10.17.1000, how do you replacing a VSF Stack Conductor Using a File-Based Feature Pack?

To replacing a VSF Stack Conductor Using a File-Based Feature Pack, follow the documented procedure: 1. Log on to the switch command-line interface 2. Trigger a switchover so member 1 is no longer the conductor.

task: Replacing a VSF Stack Conductor Using a File-Based Feature Pack

messages.content: For 4100i AOS-CX 10.17.1000, what does the guide say about Initial Configuration?

Initial Configuration: Perform the initial configuration of a factory default switch using one of the following methods: • Load a switch configuration using zero-touch provisioning (ZTP). When ZTP is used, the configuration is loaded from a server automatically when the switch booted from the factory default configuration. • Connect the management port on the switch to your network, and then use SSH client software to reach the switch from a computer connected to the same network. This requires that a DHCP server is installed on the network. Configure switch settings and features by executing CLI commands. Public SNMP-based management support 20 • Connect a computer running terminal emulation software to the console port on the switch. Configure switch settings and features by executing CLI commands. NOTE Reserved ports or ports used by other applications/services with in the system are not recommended to be used for other services. When two services use the same port there is chance of unexpected behaviors from these services. Best practices is to use unique port for each service across system.
For 4100i AOS-CX 10.17.1000, how do you configure banners?

To configure banners, follow the documented procedure: 1. Configure the banner that is displayed when a user connects to a device using a console port or in-band 2. Configure the banner that is displayed after a user is authenticated. Use the command banner exe.
For 4100i AOS-CX 10.17.1000, how do you configure the management interface?

To configure the management interface, follow the documented procedure: 1. Switch to the in-band management interface context with the command interface vlan 1 2. Use the command ip dhcp to configure the in-band management interface to automatically obtain 3. SSH is enabled by default on the default VRF. If disabled, enable SSH with the command ssh serve.
For 4100i AOS-CX 10.17.1000, what does the ntp authentication command do?
The ntp authentication command enables support for authentication when communicating with an NTP server. The no form of this command disables authentication support. Syntax: ntp authentication.
For 4100i AOS-CX 10.17.1000, what is the syntax of the ntp authentication command?
Syntax: ntp authentication
For 4100i AOS-CX 10.17.1000, what does the ntp authentication-key command do?

The ntp authentication-key command defines an authentication key that is used to secure the exchange with an NTP time server. This command provides protection against accidentally synchronizing to a time source that is not trusted. The no form of this command removes the authentication key. NOTE Using "#" in the NTP password will prevent the NTP to syncronize when authentication is enabled Public ntp authentication-key 34 Specifies the authentication key ID. Range: 1 to 65534. <KEY-ID> md5 sha1 <PLAINTXT-KEY> trusted ciphertext <ENCRYPTED-KEY> Selects MD5 key encryption. Specifies SHA1 key encryption. Specifies the plaintext authentication key. Range: 8 to 40 chara cters. The key may contain printable ASCII characters excluding "#" or be entered in hex. Keys longer than 20 characters are ass umed to be hex. Syntax: ntp authentication-key <KEY-ID> {md5 | sha1}.
For 4100i AOS-CX 10.17.1000, what is the syntax of the ntp authentication-key command?
Syntax: ntp authentication-key <KEY-ID> {md5 | sha1}
For 4100i AOS-CX 10.17.1000, what does the ntp disable command do?
The ntp disable command disables the NTP client on the switch. The NTP client is disabled by default. Syntax: ntp disable.
For 4100i AOS-CX 10.17.1000, what is the syntax of the ntp disable command?
Syntax: ntp disable
For 4100i AOS-CX 10.17.1000, what does the ntp enable command do?

The ntp enable command enables the NTP client on the switch to automatically adjust the local time and date on the switch. The NTP client is disabled by default. The no form of this command disables the NTP client. Syntax: ntp enable.
For 4100i AOS-CX 10.17.1000, what is the syntax of the ntp enable command?
Syntax: ntp enable
For 4100i AOS-CX 10.17.1000, what does the ntp server command do?

The ntp server command defines an NTP server to use for time synchronization, or updates the settings of an existing server with new values. Up to eight servers can be defined. The no form of this command removes a configured NTP server. NOTE The default NTP version is 4; it is backwards compatible with version 3. server <IP-ADDR> Specifies the address of an NTP server as a DNS name, an IPv4 address (x.x.x.x), where x is a decimal number from 0 to 255, o r an IPv6 address (xxxx: xxxx: xxxx: xxxx: xxxx: xxxx: xxxx: xxx x), where x is a hexadecimal number from 0 to F. When specifying an IPv4 address, you can remove leading zero s. For example, the address 192.169.005.100 becomes 192.16 8.5.100. Syntax: ntp server <IP-ADDR> [key <KEY-NUM>] [minpoll <MIN-NUM>] [maxpoll <MAX-NUM>].
For 4100i AOS-CX 10.17.1000, what is the syntax of the ntp server command?
Syntax: ntp server <IP-ADDR> [key <KEY-NUM>] [minpoll <MIN-NUM>] [maxpoll <MAX-NUM>]
For 4100i AOS-CX 10.17.1000, what does the clear client probe statistics command do?

The clear client probe statistics command clears the statistics associated with client probing. Specifies the interface identifier range. Clearing client probe statistics on all enabled interfaces: switch# clear client probe statistics Clearing client probe statistics on a given interface: switch# clear client probe statistics interface 1/1/1. Syntax: clear client probe statistics [interface [<IFRANGE>]].
For 4100i AOS-CX 10.17.1000, what is the syntax of the clear client probe statistics command?
Syntax: clear client probe statistics [interface [<IFRANGE>]]
For 4100i AOS-CX 10.17.1000, what does the client-probe autogen-probe-file force-regen command do?

The client-probe autogen-probe-file force-regen command forces the switch to regenerate the probe file. By default, the probe file is regenerated every 30 seconds when enabled. Syntax: client-probe autogen-probe-file force-regen.
For 4100i AOS-CX 10.17.1000, what is the syntax of the client-probe autogen-probe-file force-regen command?
Syntax: client-probe autogen-probe-file force-regen
For 4100i AOS-CX 10.17.1000, what does the client-probe profile force-download command do?

The client-probe profile force-download command enables a user to immediately force download a remote profile rather than waiting until the next cycle of remote-server refresh-interval timer. Syntax: client-probe profile force-download.
For 4100i AOS-CX 10.17.1000, what is the syntax of the client-probe profile force-download command?
Syntax: client-probe profile force-download
For 4100i AOS-CX 10.17.1000, what does the client probe enable command do?
The client probe enable command enables client probing on a port. Client probing must also be enabled globally for it to be operational. Syntax: client probe enable.
For 4100i AOS-CX 10.17.1000, what is the syntax of the client probe enable command?
Syntax: client probe enable
For 4100i AOS-CX 10.17.1000, what does the probe-interval <ID> command do?

The probe-interval <ID> command configures the time period, in seconds, for the switch to wait before probing for clients again. Specifies the interval between consecutive probes, in seconds. Default: 60 seconds. Configuring the switch to send probe packets every 30 seconds: switch(config)# client probe switch(config-cp)# probe-interval 30. Syntax: probe-interval <ID>.
For 4100i AOS-CX 10.17.1000, what is the syntax of the probe-interval <ID> command?
Syntax: probe-interval <ID>
For 4100i AOS-CX 10.17.1000, what does the probe-type {ping-broadcast | ping-unicast | ping-sweep | garp} command do?

The probe-type {ping-broadcast | ping-unicast | ping-sweep | garp} command configures up to multiple packet types to use for probing. The switch sends a packet of each configured type per probe cycle. Syntax: probe-type {ping-broadcast | ping-unicast | ping-sweep | garp}.
For 4100i AOS-CX 10.17.1000, what is the syntax of the probe-type {ping-broadcast | ping-unicast | ping-sweep | garp} command?
Syntax: probe-type {ping-broadcast | ping-unicast | ping-sweep | garp}
For 4100i AOS-CX 10.17.1000, what does the profile <PROFILE-NAME> command do?
The profile <PROFILE-NAME> command creates a local or remote profile and enters the profile context. Specifies the name of the profile. Public (local) profile 62. Syntax: profile <PROFILE-NAME>.
For 4100i AOS-CX 10.17.1000, what is the syntax of the profile <PROFILE-NAME> command?
Syntax: profile <PROFILE-NAME>
For 4100i AOS-CX 10.17.1000, what does the remote-server refresh interval <ID> command do?

The remote-server refresh interval <ID> command configures the time period, in seconds, when the switch will periodically obtain the latest version of the probe profile file from the remote location. Public remote-server refresh interval 63 Time interval, in seconds, to wait before refreshing the client p robing data from the source. Default: 3600 seconds Configuring the switch to refresh the probe data from the remote location every 3600 seconds: switch(config)# client probe switch(config-cp)# remote-server refresh-interval 3600. Syntax: remote-server refresh interval <ID>.
For 4100i AOS-CX 10.17.1000, what is the syntax of the remote-server refresh interval <ID> command?
Syntax: remote-server refresh interval <ID>
For 4100i AOS-CX 10.17.1000, what does the show client probe statistics command do?

The show client probe statistics command displays the statistics associated with client probing. Public show client probe statistics 64 Specifies the interface identifier range. Showing client probe statistics on all enabled interfaces: switch# show client probe statistics Client-Probe Statistics. Syntax: show client probe statistics [interface <IFRANGE>].
For 4100i AOS-CX 10.17.1000, what is the syntax of the show client probe statistics command?
Syntax: show client probe statistics [interface <IFRANGE>]
For 4100i AOS-CX 10.17.1000, what does the flow-control rxtx command do?

The flow-control rxtx command command flow-control enables negotiation of IEEE 802.3x link-level flow control on the current interface. The switch advertises link-level flow control support to the link partner. The final configuration is determined based on the capabilities of both partners. Each invocation of this command replaces the previous configuration. The no form of these commands disables any configured flow control on the selected interface. Public flow-control 92 Enables the ability to honor received and to transmit IEEE 802. 3x LLFC pause frames to the remote device. • For interfaces that auto-negotiate, link-level flow control is subject to negotiation, plus speed and other parameters. Both ends of the link must negotiate the same flow control mode for it to be applied. Syntax: flow-control rxtx.
For 4100i AOS-CX 10.17.1000, what is the syntax of the flow-control rxtx command?
Syntax: flow-control rxtx
For 4100i AOS-CX 10.17.1000, what does the interface command do?

The interface command switches to the config-if context for a physical port. This is where you define the configuration settings for the logical interface associated with the physical port. Specifies a physical port number. Format: member/slot/port. <PORT-NUM>. Syntax: interface <PORT-NUM>.
For 4100i AOS-CX 10.17.1000, what is the syntax of the interface command?
Syntax: interface <PORT-NUM>
For 4100i AOS-CX 10.17.1000, what does the no interface vlan <VLAN-ID> command do?

The no interface vlan <VLAN-ID> command creates an interface VLAN also know as an SVI (switched virtual interface) and changes to the config-if- vlan context. The specified VLAN must already be defined on the switch. The no form of this command deletes an interface VLAN. Specifies the interface ID.
For 4100i AOS-CX 10.17.1000, what does the ip address command do?

The ip address command sets an IP/IPv6 address on the interface VLAN. The no form of this command removes the IP/IPv6 address from the interface. Specifies an IP address in IPv4 format (x.x.x.x), where x is a de cimal number from 0 to 255. You can remove leading zeros. For Public ip address 96 example, the address 192.169.005.100 becomes 192.168.5.1 00. Specifies the number of bits in the address mask in CIDR format (x), where x is a decimal number from 0 to 128. Syntax: ip address <IP-ADDR>/<MASK>.
For 4100i AOS-CX 10.17.1000, what does the ip mtu command do?

The ip mtu command sets the IP MTU (maximum transmission unit) for an interface. This defines the largest IP packet that can be sent or received by the interface. This value should be less than or equal to the overall MTU for the interface. The no form of this command sets the IP MTU to the default value 1500. Specifies the IP MTU in bytes. Range: 68 to 9198. Default: 150 0. <VALUE>. Syntax: ip mtu <VALUE>.
For 4100i AOS-CX 10.17.1000, what is the syntax of the ip mtu command?
Syntax: ip mtu <VALUE>
For 4100i AOS-CX 10.17.1000, what is the syntax of the IPv6 address command?
Syntax: IPv6 address <IPv6-ADDR>/<MASK>{eui64 | [tag <ID>]}
For 4100i AOS-CX 10.17.1000, what does the IPv6 tcp mss command do?

The IPv6 tcp mss command sets the TCP (Transmission Control Protocol) MSS (Maximum Segment Size) for an interface. When configured, the MSS option in the TCP header is set in the TCP handshake for negotiation during connection establishment. Once the TCP session is established, changing the TCP MSS value on the interface will not affect the existing session. The no form of this command removes the TCP MSS configuration from the interface. NOTE TCP MSS is supported on ROP, SVI, subinterface, and L3 GRE tunnel interface. Specifies the IPv6 TCP MSS in bytes. Range: 68 to 65495. Public IPv6 tcp mss 104. Syntax: IPv6 tcp mss <VALUE>.
For 4100i AOS-CX 10.17.1000, what is the syntax of the IPv6 tcp mss command?
Syntax: IPv6 tcp mss <VALUE>
For 4100i AOS-CX 10.17.1000, what does the persona {access | uplink | custom <PERSONA-NAME>} [copy | attach] command do?

The persona {access | uplink | custom <PERSONA-NAME>} [copy | attach] command associates one of three persona types with an interface to classify the purpose or role of an interface. On the 10000 Switch Series, “access” persona ports are typically connected to workloads / VMs, and the “uplink” (fabric) persona ports are connected to the core / spine. The no form of this command removes the interface persona. Selects the access persona type. Selects the uplink persona type. custom <PERSONA-NAME> Selects the custom persona type with a user-provided name. Range: 1 to 64 printable ASCII characters including space. copy attach. Syntax: persona {access | uplink | custom <PERSONA-NAME>} [copy | attach].
For 4100i AOS-CX 10.17.1000, what is the syntax of the persona {access | uplink | custom <PERSONA-NAME>} [copy | attach] command?
Syntax: persona {access | uplink | custom <PERSONA-NAME>} [copy | attach]
For 4100i AOS-CX 10.17.1000, what does the show allow-unsupported-transceiver command do?
The show allow-unsupported-transceiver command displays configuration and status of unsupported transceivers. Syntax: show allow-unsupported-transceiver.
For 4100i AOS-CX 10.17.1000, what is the syntax of the show allow-unsupported-transceiver command?
Syntax: show allow-unsupported-transceiver
For 4100i AOS-CX 10.17.1000, what does the show interface command do?

The show interface command shows active configurations and operational status information for interfaces. Specifies a interface name. <IFNAME> <IFRANGE> Specifies the port identifier range. Public show interface 112 Shows brief info in tabular format. Shows the physical connection info in tabular format. Shows additional statistics, including the tx filtered and rx filte red counters. • Rx filter packets are protocol packets received when the p rotocol is disabled on the switch and there is only one port i n the VLAN. Protocols include OSPF, PIM, RIP, LACP, and LL DP. • An example of a Tx filtered packet would be a multicast pac ket being filtered from going out of the ingress port. Shows statistics rounded to the nearest power of 1000, for exa mple, 1K, 345M, 2G. Syntax: show interface [<IFNNAME>|<IFRANGE>] [brief | physical].
For 4100i AOS-CX 10.17.1000, what is the syntax of the show interface command?
Syntax: show interface [<IFNNAME>|<IFRANGE>] [brief | physical]
For 4100i AOS-CX 10.17.1000, what does the show interface dom command do?

The show interface dom command shows diagnostics information and alarm/warning flags for the optical transceivers (SFP, SFP+). This information is known as DOM (Digital Optical Monitoring). DOM information also consists of vendor determined thresholds which trigger high/low alarms and warning flags. Specifies an interface. Format: member/slot/port. <INTERFACE-ID> detail. Syntax: show interface [<INTERFACE-ID>] dom [detail].
For 4100i AOS-CX 10.17.1000, what is the syntax of the show interface dom command?
Syntax: show interface [<INTERFACE-ID>] dom [detail]
For 4100i AOS-CX 10.17.1000, what is the syntax of the show interface flow-control command?
Syntax: show interface [<IFNNAME>|<IFRANGE>] flow-control [detail]
For 4100i AOS-CX 10.17.1000, what does the show interface link-diagnostics command do?

The show interface link-diagnostics command shows information about select link diagnostics, including MAC, PHY, and Transceiver diagnostics. Specifies a interface name. <IFNAME> Public show interface link-diagnostics 132 Specifies the port identifier range. <IFRANGE> link-diagnostics Shows link diagnostics information. Syntax: show interface [<IFNNAME>|<IFRANGE>] link-diagnostics.
For 4100i AOS-CX 10.17.1000, what is the syntax of the show interface link-diagnostics command?
Syntax: show interface [<IFNNAME>|<IFRANGE>] link-diagnostics
For 4100i AOS-CX 10.17.1000, what does the show interface statistics command do?

The show interface statistics command shows statistics for switch interfaces such as packets transmitted and received, bytes transmitted and received, broadcast and multicast packets. Specifies a interface name. <IFNAME> <IFRANGE> LAG <LAG-ID> monitor Specifies the port identifier range. Shows LAG interface information. Specifies the LAG number. Range: 1-256 Continuously monitor interface statistics. human-readable Shows statistics rounded to the nearest power of 1000, for exa mple, 1K, 345M, 2G. Shows only non zero statistics. non-zero. Syntax: show interface [<IFNAME>|<IFRANGE>] statistics [non-zero] [human-readable].
For 4100i AOS-CX 10.17.1000, what is the syntax of the show interface statistics command?
Syntax: show interface [<IFNAME>|<IFRANGE>] statistics [non-zero] [human-readable]
For 4100i AOS-CX 10.17.1000, what is the syntax of the show interface transceiver command?
Syntax: show interface [<INTERFACE-ID>] transceiver [detail | threshold-violations]
For 4100i AOS-CX 10.17.1000, what does the show interface supported-transceivers command do?

The show interface supported-transceivers command displays transceivers supported by a specific, or all, interface(s) in the switch. Information varies and is dependent on transceiver types, part number, and short description or interfaces. Specifies the name of an interface on the switch. Use the following format: member/slot/port. Syntax: show interface {<IFNAME>|<IFRANGE>} supported-transceivers.
For 4100i AOS-CX 10.17.1000, what is the syntax of the show interface supported-transceivers command?
Syntax: show interface {<IFNAME>|<IFRANGE>} supported-transceivers
For 4100i AOS-CX 10.17.1000, what does the shutdown command do?
The shutdown command disables an interface. Interfaces are disabled by default when created. The no form of this command enables an interface. Syntax: shutdown.
For 4100i AOS-CX 10.17.1000, what is the syntax of the shutdown command?
Syntax: shutdown
For 4100i AOS-CX 10.17.1000, what does the guide say about VLANs?

VLANs: Public Source interface selection 163 VLANs are primarily used to provide network segmentation at layer 2. VLANs enable the grouping of users by logical function instead of physical location. They make managing bandwidth usage within networks possible by: • Allowing grouping of high-bandwidth users on low-traffic segments • Organizing users from different LAN segments according to their need for common resources and individual protocols • Improving traffic control at the edge of networks by separating traffic of different protocol types. • Enhancing network security by creating subnets to control in-band access to specific network resources VLANs are generally assigned on an organizational basis rather than on a physical basis. For example, a network administrator could assign all workstations and servers used by a particular workgroup to the same VLAN, regardless of their physical locations. Hosts in the same VLAN can directly communicate with one another. A router or a Layer 3 switch is required for hosts in different VLANs to communicate with one another.
For 4100i AOS-CX 10.17.1000, what does the checkpoint auto confirm command do?

The checkpoint auto confirm command signals to the switch to save the running configuration used during the auto checkpoint mode. This command also ends the auto checkpoint mode. Public checkpoint auto confirm 173. Syntax: checkpoint auto confirm.
For 4100i AOS-CX 10.17.1000, what is the syntax of the checkpoint auto confirm command?
Syntax: checkpoint auto confirm
For 4100i AOS-CX 10.17.1000, what is the syntax of the checkpoint diff command?
Syntax: checkpoint diff {<CHECKPOINT-NAME1> | running-config | startup-config}
For 4100i AOS-CX 10.17.1000, what is the syntax of the checkpoint post-configuration timeout command?
Syntax: checkpoint post-configuration timeout <TIMEOUT>
For 4100i AOS-CX 10.17.1000, what does the checkpoint rename command do?

The checkpoint rename command renames an existing checkpoint. <OLD-CHECKPOINT-NAME> Specifies the name of an existing checkpoint to be renamed. <NEW-CHECKPOINT-NAME> Specifies the new name for the checkpoint. The checkpoint nam e can be alphanumeric. It can also contain underscores (_) and dashes (-). NOTE Do not start the checkpoint name with CPC be cause it is used for system-generated checkpo ints. Public checkpoint rename 181. Syntax: checkpoint rename <OLD-CHECKPOINT-NAME>.
For 4100i AOS-CX 10.17.1000, what is the syntax of the checkpoint rename command?
Syntax: checkpoint rename <OLD-CHECKPOINT-NAME>
For 4100i AOS-CX 10.17.1000, what is the syntax of the checkpoint rollback command?
Syntax: checkpoint rollback {<CHECKPOINT-NAME> | startup-config}
For 4100i AOS-CX 10.17.1000, what does the copy startup-config running-config command do?
The copy startup-config running-config command copies the startup configuration to the running configuration. Syntax: copy startup-config running-config.
For 4100i AOS-CX 10.17.1000, what is the syntax of the copy startup-config running-config command?
Syntax: copy startup-config running-config
For 4100i AOS-CX 10.17.1000, what does the write memory command do?

The write memory command saves the running configuration to the startup configuration. It is an alias of the command copy running- config startup-config. If the startup configuration is already present, this command overwrites the startup configuration. Public write memory 210. Syntax: write memory.
For 4100i AOS-CX 10.17.1000, what is the syntax of the write memory command?
Syntax: write memory
For 4100i AOS-CX 10.17.1000, what does the guide say about Configuration version migration?

Configuration version migration: A configuration version migration occurred which re quired a reboot. Console error Fabric fault The console failed to start. A fabric fault occurred. All line modules faulted A zero line card condition occurred. Redundancy switchover requested A user requested a redundancy switchover.
For 4100i AOS-CX 10.17.1000, what does the guide say about Redundant Management election timeout?

Redundant Management election timeout: A failure to elect a standby management module in t he allotted time. Public show boot-history 216 Boot History String Critical service fault (error) Description A daemon critical to switch operation has stopped fu nctioning. An extra error string may be present to d escribe the error in detail. VSF autojoin renumber Reset triggered by VSF autojoin. VSF member renumbered A user requested a renumber of a VSF member. VSF switchover requested A user requested a VSF switchover.
For 4100i AOS-CX 10.17.1000, what does the guide say about VSX software update?

VSX software update: Reset triggered by a VSX software update. Chassis critical temperature Chassis operating temperature exceeded. Chassis low critical temperature Chassis temperature below the minimum operating threshold. Chassis insufficient fans Insufficient fans to cool the chassis. Chassis unsupported PSUs/fans Unsupported or misconfigured PSUs or system fans.
For 4100i AOS-CX 10.17.1000, what does the copy {primary secondary} <REMOTE-URL> command do?

The copy {primary secondary} <REMOTE-URL> command uploads a firmware image to a TFTP or SFTP server. {primary | secondary} Selects the primary or secondary image profile to upload. Requi red Specifies the URL to receive the uploaded firmware using SFTP, TFTP or SCP. TFTP format: Public Firmware management commands 220 tftp://<IP-ADDR>[: <PORT-NUM>] [; blocksize=<Value>]/<FILENAME> SFTP format: sftp://<USERNAME>@<IP-ADDR> [: <PORT-NUM>]/<FILENAME> SCP format: scp://USER@{IP|HOST}[: PORT]/FILE vrf <VRF-NAME> Specifies a VRF name. Default: default. Syntax: copy {primary | secondary} <REMOTE-URL> [vrf <VRF-NAME>].
For 4100i AOS-CX 10.17.1000, what is the syntax of the copy {primary secondary} <REMOTE-URL> command?
Syntax: copy {primary | secondary} <REMOTE-URL> [vrf <VRF-NAME>]
For 4100i AOS-CX 10.17.1000, what is the syntax of the copy <STORAGE-URL> 227 command?
Syntax: copy <STORAGE-URL> {hot-patch|primary|secondary}
For 4100i AOS-CX 10.17.1000, what does the hot-patch apply|remove <name.patch> command do?

The hot-patch apply|remove <name.patch> command apply hot-patch software or remove it from the switch. The no form of the hot-patch apply command disables the hot-patch image, but does not remove it from the switch. Rebooting the system after disabling or removing the patch is not required. Profile names apply <name.patch> Apply the specified hot-patch image to a standalone switch or VSF stack. AOS-CX hot-patch software images can be obtaine Public hot-patch 230 Profile names d from HPE Aruba Networking customer support, and are identif ied with a.patch extension. Disables the hot-patch image and removes the patch from the switch. This removal will also disable the patch. Once removed, a hot-patch must be downloaded again in order to be applied. remove <name.patch>. Syntax: hot-patch apply|remove <name.patch>.
For 4100i AOS-CX 10.17.1000, what is the syntax of the hot-patch apply|remove <name.patch> command?
Syntax: hot-patch apply|remove <name.patch>
For 4100i AOS-CX 10.17.1000, how do you configure SNMP?

To configure SNMP, follow the documented procedure: 1. Enable SNMP on a VRF using the command snmp-server vrf default 2. Set the system contact, location, and description for the switch with the following commands: .

section: Configuring HPE Aruba Networking Central location

messages.content: For 4100i AOS-CX 10.17.1000, how do you configure HPE Aruba Networking Central location?

To configure HPE Aruba Networking Central location, follow the documented procedure: 1. Create the hpe-anw-central context 2. Configure the HPE Aruba Networking Central server location 3. Validate the connectivity status to HPE Aruba Networking Central.

task: Configuring HPE Aruba Networking Central location

messages.content: For 4100i AOS-CX 10.17.1000, how do you configure DNS server address?
To configure DNS server address, follow the documented procedure: 1. Configure the DNS server address 2. Validate the DNS server status.
For 4100i AOS-CX 10.17.1000, what does the NOTE command do?

The NOTE command configures the device to only be writable from HPE Aruba Networking Central. HPE Aruba Networking Central will be the only agent that can add, modify, or delete configurations on the device. The no form of this command disables this feature. NOTE The no form of this command is only available when the device is disconnected from HPE Aruba Networking Central. Syntax: NOTE.
For 4100i AOS-CX 10.17.1000, what is the syntax of the NOTE command?
Syntax: NOTE
For 4100i AOS-CX 10.17.1000, what does the ip dns domain-name command do?

The ip dns domain-name command configures a domain name that is appended to the DNS request. The domain can be either IPv4 or IPv6. By default, requests are forwarded on the default VRF. If a domain list is defined with the command ip dns domain-list, the domain name defined with this command is ignored. The no form of this command removes the domain name. Specifies the domain name to append to DNS requests. Length: 1 to 256 characters. <DOMAIN-NAME> vrf <VRF-NAME> Specifies a VRF name. Default: default. Syntax: ip dns domain-name <DOMAIN-NAME> [ vrf <VRF-NAME> ].
For 4100i AOS-CX 10.17.1000, what is the syntax of the ip dns domain-name command?
Syntax: ip dns domain-name <DOMAIN-NAME> [ vrf <VRF-NAME> ]
For 4100i AOS-CX 10.17.1000, what does the ip dns host command do?

The ip dns host command associates a static IP address with a hostname. The DNS client returns this IP address instead of querying a DNS server for an IP address for the hostname. Up to six hosts can be defined. If no VRF is defined, the default VRF is used. The no form of this command removes a static IP address associated with a hostname. host <HOST-NAME> Specifies the name of a host. Length: 1 to 256 characters. <IP-ADDR> Specifies an IP address in IPv4 format (x.x.x.x), where x is a de cimal number from 0 to 255, or IPv6 format (xxxx: xxxx: x xxx: xxxx: xxxx: xxxx: xxxx: xxxx), where x is a hexa decimal number from 0 to F. vrf <VRF-NAME> Specifies a VRF name. Default: default. Syntax: ip dns host <HOST-NAME>.
For 4100i AOS-CX 10.17.1000, what is the syntax of the ip dns host command?
Syntax: ip dns host <HOST-NAME>

section: Configuring a device profile for LLDP

messages.content: For 4100i AOS-CX 10.17.1000, how do you configure a device profile for LLDP?

To configure a device profile for LLDP, follow the documented procedure: 1. Create an LLDP group with the command port-access lldp-group 2. Define rules for adding devices to an LLDP group with the command match 3. Define rules for ignoring devices so that they are not added to an LLDP group with the command ign 4. Create a device profile with the command port-access device-profile 5. Add the LLDP group with the command associate lldp-group 6. Add a role to a device profile with the command associate role. Make sure that the role is 7. Enable the device profile with the command enable.

task: Configuring a device profile for LLDP

section: Configuring a device profile for CDP

messages.content: For 4100i AOS-CX 10.17.1000, how do you configure a device profile for CDP?

To configure a device profile for CDP, follow the documented procedure: 1. Create a CDP group with the command port-access cdp-group 2. Define rules for adding devices to a CDP group with the command match 3. Define rules for ignoring devices so that they are not added to a CDP group with the command ignor 4. Create a device profile with the command port-access device-profile 5. Add a CDP group to a device profile with the command associate cdp-group 6. Add a role to a device profile with the command associate role. Make sure that the role is 7. Enable a device profile with the command enable.

task: Configuring a device profile for CDP

section: Configuring a device profile for local MAC match

messages.content: For 4100i AOS-CX 10.17.1000, how do you configure a device profile for local MAC match?

To configure a device profile for local MAC match, follow the documented procedure: 1. Create a MAC group with the mac-group command 2. Define rules for adding devices to a MAC group with the match (for MAC groups) command.

task: Configuring a device profile for local MAC match

messages.content: For 4100i AOS-CX 10.17.1000, what does the associate mac-group <GROUP-NAME> command do?

The associate mac-group <GROUP-NAME> command associates a MAC group with a device profile. A maximum of two MAC groups can be associated with a device profile. The no form of this command removes a MAC group from a device profile. Specifies the name of the MAC group to associate with this devi ce profile. Range: 1 to 32 alphanumeric characters. <GROUP-NAME>. Syntax: associate mac-group <GROUP-NAME>.
For 4100i AOS-CX 10.17.1000, what is the syntax of the associate mac-group <GROUP-NAME> command?
Syntax: associate mac-group <GROUP-NAME>
For 4100i AOS-CX 10.17.1000, what does the associate role <ROLE-NAME> command do?

The associate role <ROLE-NAME> command associates a role with a device profile. Only one role can be associated with a device profile. For information on how to configure a role, see the port access role information in the Security Guide. The no form of this command removes a role from a device profile. Specifies the name of the role to associate with the device profil e. Range: 1 to 64 alphanumeric characters. <ROLE-NAME>. Syntax: associate role <ROLE-NAME>.
For 4100i AOS-CX 10.17.1000, what is the syntax of the associate role <ROLE-NAME> command?
Syntax: associate role <ROLE-NAME>
For 4100i AOS-CX 10.17.1000, what does the disable command do?
The disable command disables a device profile. The no form of this command enables a device profile. Syntax: disable.
For 4100i AOS-CX 10.17.1000, what is the syntax of the disable command?
Syntax: disable
For 4100i AOS-CX 10.17.1000, what does the enable command do?
The enable command enables a device profile. The no form of this command disables a device profile. Syntax: enable.
For 4100i AOS-CX 10.17.1000, what is the syntax of the enable command?
Syntax: enable
For 4100i AOS-CX 10.17.1000, what does the port-access device-profile <DEVICE-PROFILE-NAME> command do?

The port-access device-profile <DEVICE-PROFILE-NAME> command creates a new device profile and switches to the config-device-profile context. A maximum of 32 device profiles can be created. This command can be issued from the interface (config-if) or Link Aggregation Group (config-lag-if) contexts. The no form of this command removes a device profile. Specifies the name of a device profile. Range: 1 to 32 alphanum eric characters. <DEVICE-PROFILE-NAME>. Syntax: port-access device-profile <DEVICE-PROFILE-NAME>.
For 4100i AOS-CX 10.17.1000, what is the syntax of the port-access device-profile <DEVICE-PROFILE-NAME> command?
Syntax: port-access device-profile <DEVICE-PROFILE-NAME>
For 4100i AOS-CX 10.17.1000, what restrictions or notes are documented for Guidelines?

For Guidelines, the guide documents the following restrictions, notes, or considerations: • An LLDP must not contain more than one EEE TLV. Public LLDP MED support 332 • LLDP may or may not be enabled on the interfaces supporting EEE. If LLDP is not enabled, it might not be in the optimal operational mode. • EEE TLV should not be exchanged until it negotiates from L1 and it detects peer EEE capabilities. • EEE TLV is disabled by default and enabled only when EEE is active. • EEE and EEE TLV exchange can be enabled or disabled at the interface level.
For 4100i AOS-CX 10.17.1000, how do you configure the LLDP agent?

To configure the LLDP agent, follow the documented procedure: 1. By default, the LLDP agent sets the management address in all TLVs in the following order: 2. By default, all supported TLVs are sent and received. To customize the list, use the command lldp s 3. By default, support for the LLDP-MED TLV is enabled. To customize settings, use the commands lldp.
For 4100i AOS-CX 10.17.1000, what does the clear lldp neighbors command do?
The clear lldp neighbors command clears all LLDP neighbor details. Public LLDP commands 334. Syntax: clear lldp neighbors.
For 4100i AOS-CX 10.17.1000, what is the syntax of the clear lldp neighbors command?
Syntax: clear lldp neighbors
For 4100i AOS-CX 10.17.1000, what does the clear lldp statistics command do?
The clear lldp statistics command clears all LLDP neighbor statistics. Syntax: clear lldp statistics.
For 4100i AOS-CX 10.17.1000, what is the syntax of the clear lldp statistics command?
Syntax: clear lldp statistics
For 4100i AOS-CX 10.17.1000, what does the lldp command do?

The lldp command enables LLDP support globally on all active interfaces. By default, LLDP is enabled. The no form of this command disables LLDP support globally on all active interfaces. It does not remove any LLDP configuration settings. Syntax: lldp.
For 4100i AOS-CX 10.17.1000, what is the syntax of the lldp command?
Syntax: lldp
For 4100i AOS-CX 10.17.1000, what does the lldp dot3 mfs command do?

The lldp dot3 mfs command enables the 802.3 TLV list in LLDP to advertise for maximum frame size (MFS). Enabled by default. The no form of this command disables the advertisement of maximum frame size TLVs. Syntax: lldp dot3 mfs.
For 4100i AOS-CX 10.17.1000, what is the syntax of the lldp dot3 mfs command?
Syntax: lldp dot3 mfs
For 4100i AOS-CX 10.17.1000, what does the no lldp management-address vlan <VLAN-ID> command do?

The no lldp management-address vlan <VLAN-ID> command sets the VLAN whose IPv4 or IPv6 address is advertised as the LLDP management authority. The no form of this command removes the VLAN whose IPv4 or IPv6 address is advertised as the LLDP management authority. The following is the precedence for the management IP address TLV in the LLDP packet (in order): Public lldp management-address vlan 341 • LLDP management-IP-address and management-IPv6-address, if configured. • LLDP management VLAN's IPv4 and IPv6 address, if configured. • Loopback IP address from the smallest configured loopback interface identifier. • Route-only-port IP address (Layer-3 interface) or IP address of the SVI (Layer-2 interface). • OOBM IP address. • Base MAC address of the switch. Specifies the VLAN ID.
For 4100i AOS-CX 10.17.1000, what does the lldp management-ip-address <IPV-ADDR> command do?

The lldp management-ip-address <IPV-ADDR> command defines the IP management address of the switch which is sent in the management address TLV. One IPv4 and one IPv6 management address can be configured. If you do not define an LLDP management address, then LLDP uses one of the following (in order): •. Syntax: lldp management-ip-address <IPV-ADDR>.
For 4100i AOS-CX 10.17.1000, what is the syntax of the lldp management-ip-address <IPV-ADDR> command?
Syntax: lldp management-ip-address <IPV-ADDR>
For 4100i AOS-CX 10.17.1000, what does the IP address of SVI [interface VLAN <vid>] command do?
The IP address of SVI [interface VLAN <vid>] command specifies the management address of the switch as an IPv4 for mat (x.x.x.x), where x is a decimal value from 0 to 255. <IPv4-ADDR>.
For 4100i AOS-CX 10.17.1000, what does the ztp force-provision command do?
The ztp force-provision command starts on-demand ZTP via DHCP. Syntax: ztp force-provision.
For 4100i AOS-CX 10.17.1000, what is the syntax of the ztp force-provision command?
Syntax: ztp force-provision
For 4100i AOS-CX 10.17.1000, what does the guide say about Thermal Management?

Thermal Management: consists of the fand and tempd daemons, that manage the fans and temperature sensors for the system respectively. They are responsible for monitoring temperatures, adjusting fan speeds to keep the system at nominal temperatures, initiating shutdown sequences for chassis, subsystems, or transceivers during over-temperature conditions, and reporting temperatures, sensor states, fan speeds, and fan states. Subtopics Thermal management support.
For 4100i AOS-CX 10.17.1000, what does the guide say about Thermal Shutdown Behavior?

Thermal Shutdown Behavior: Chassis (Switch) Thermal Shutdow - Thermal shutdown is a critical sa fety mechanism designed to preve nt hardware damage caused by overheating. When critical tempe rature thresholds are exceeded, th ermal daemons log an event and i nitiate a shutdown sequence to po wer down the affected componen ts gracefully. If a temperature sensor exceeds its critical threshold and the associate d subsystem does not support bei ng powered off, the entire chassis will shut down for a five minute c ool down period. If the critical tem perature is detected again after re boot, the shutdown sequence will repeat. Platforms 8325H 9300-32C8D 9300-32D All platforms All platforms Public.
For 4100i AOS-CX 10.17.1000, what does the guide say about Transceiver Thermal Management?

Transceiver Thermal Management: Transceiver temperature sensors are visible on REST API calls, SNMP and CLI (show environment temperature). Events logs also are triggered upon thermal status changes, shutdown and recovery. If a transceiver exceeds the temperature threshold and it is hot swapped, the cool time timer is canceled and the transceiver is re-enabled. There is no limit to the number of times a transceiver is thermally disabled and re-enabled if it's continuously above the critical temperature threshold. This is an example of the temperature information on the normal conditions. switch# show environment temperature Temperature information ---------------------------------------------------------------------------- -- Current.
For 4100i AOS-CX 10.17.1000, what does the clear events command do?
The clear events command clears up event logs. Using the show events command will only display the logs generated after the c lear events command. Syntax: clear events.
For 4100i AOS-CX 10.17.1000, what is the syntax of the clear events command?
Syntax: clear events
For 4100i AOS-CX 10.17.1000, what does the console baud-rate <SPEED> command do?

The console baud-rate <SPEED> command sets the console serial port speed. The no form of this command resets the console port speed to its default of 115200 bps. Selects the console port speed in bps, either 9600 or 1152 00. <SPEED>. Syntax: console baud-rate <SPEED>.
For 4100i AOS-CX 10.17.1000, what is the syntax of the console baud-rate <SPEED> command?
Syntax: console baud-rate <SPEED>
For 4100i AOS-CX 10.17.1000, what does the hostname <HOSTNAME> command do?

The hostname <HOSTNAME> command sets the host name of the switch. The no form of this command sets the host name to the default value, which is switch. Specifies the host name. The first character of the host name m ust be a letter or a number. Length: 1 to 32 characters. Default: switch <HOSTNAME>. Syntax: hostname <HOSTNAME>.
For 4100i AOS-CX 10.17.1000, what is the syntax of the hostname <HOSTNAME> command?
Syntax: hostname <HOSTNAME>
For 4100i AOS-CX 10.17.1000, what does the show capacities command do?

The show capacities command shows system capacities and their values for all features or a specific feature. Specifies a feature. For example, aaa. <FEATURE> Public show capacities 419. Syntax: show capacities <FEATURE>.
For 4100i AOS-CX 10.17.1000, what is the syntax of the show capacities command?
Syntax: show capacities <FEATURE>
For 4100i AOS-CX 10.17.1000, what does the show capacities-status command do?

The show capacities-status command shows system capacities status and their values for all features or a specific feature. Specifies the feature, for example aaa or vrrp for which to displ ay capacities, values, and status. Required. <FEATURE>. Syntax: show capacities-status <FEATURE>.
For 4100i AOS-CX 10.17.1000, what is the syntax of the show capacities-status command?
Syntax: show capacities-status <FEATURE>
For 4100i AOS-CX 10.17.1000, what does the guide say about Number of VLANs currently configured?

Number of VLANs currently configured: 1 512 Showing the system capacities status for BGP: switch# show capacities-status bgp System Capacities Status: Filter BGP Capacities Status Name Value Maximum ---------------------------------------------------------------------------- --- Number of aspath-lists configured 0 64 Public show capacities-status 431 Number of community-lists configured 0 64 Number of neighbors configured across all VRFs 0 50 Number of peer groups configured across all VRFs 0 25 Number of prefix-lists configured 0 64 Number of route-maps configured 0 64 Number of routes in BGP RIB 0 256000 Number of route reflector clients configured across all VRFs 0 16.
For 4100i AOS-CX 10.17.1000, what does the show console command do?
The show console command shows the serial console port current speed. Syntax: show console.
For 4100i AOS-CX 10.17.1000, what is the syntax of the show console command?
Syntax: show console
For 4100i AOS-CX 10.17.1000, what does the show core-dump command do?

The show core-dump command shows core dump information about the specified module. When no parameters are specified, shows only the core dumps generated in the current boot of the management module. When the all parameter is specified, shows all available core dumps. Shows all available core dumps. When no parameters are specified, the show core-dump command shows only the core dumps generated in the current boot of the management module. You can use this command to determine when any crashes are occurring in the current boot. If no core dumps have occurred, the following message is displayed: No core dumps are present To show core dump information for the standby management module, you must use the standby command to switch to the standby management module and then execute the show core-dump command. Syntax: show core-dump.
For 4100i AOS-CX 10.17.1000, what is the syntax of the show core-dump command?
Syntax: show core-dump
For 4100i AOS-CX 10.17.1000, what does the show environment power-consumption command do?

The show environment power-consumption command displays power consumption information. Displays detailed power consumption information. For VSF supported platforms only. Displays the power consum ption information for the specified VSF member. Range: 1-10. Power consumed values are updated every 5 seconds. The total power consumed is the total power used in a chassis. The power consumed average is calculated from the total power consumed as a running average over a period of time. The average period has a default of 10 minutes. The period can be configured using power-consumption-average-period. On chassis where power input is not supported, power consumed is displayed as Power Usage and PSU Output Power is displayed as N/A. Syntax: show environment power-consumption [detail].
For 4100i AOS-CX 10.17.1000, what is the syntax of the show environment power-consumption command?
Syntax: show environment power-consumption [detail]
For 4100i AOS-CX 10.17.1000, what does the show environment power-supply command do?

The show environment power-supply command shows status information about all power supplies in the switch. Shows the member and slot number of the power supply. Product Number Shows the product number of the power supply. Serial Number Shows the serial number of the power supply, which uniquely id entifies the power supply. PSU Status The status of the power supply. Values are: Here is your list with the numbers removed: • OK: power supply is operating normally • OK*: power supply is operating normally, although there is i nsufficient power to fully power the chassis. Syntax: show environment power-supply.
For 4100i AOS-CX 10.17.1000, what is the syntax of the show environment power-supply command?
Syntax: show environment power-supply

Mbr/Slot-Sensor Module Type temperature Status: ---------------------------------------------------------------------------- -- 1/1-PHY-01-04 line-card-module 45.00 C normal 1/1-PHY-05-08 line-card-module 45.00 C normal 1/1-PHY-09-12 line-card-module 46.00 C normal 1/1-PHY-13-16 line-card-module 47.00 C normal 1/1-PHY-17-20 line-card-module 47.00 C normal 1/1-PHY-21-24 line-card-module 50.00 C normal 1/1-PHY-25-28 line-card-module 45.00 C normal 1/1-PHY-29-32 line-card-module 47.00 C normal 1/1-PHY-33-36 line-card-module 48.00 C normal 1/1-PHY-37-40 line-card-module 47.00 C normal 1/1-PHY-41-44 line-card-module 48.00 C normal 1/1-PHY-45-48 line-card-module 49.00 C normal 1/1-Switch-ASIC-Internal line-card-module 56.25 C normal 1/1-CPU-Zone-0 management-module 50.00 C normal 1/1-CPU-Zone-1 management-module 50.00 C normal Public show environment temperature 456 1/1-CPU-Zone-2 management-module 50.00 C normal 1/1-CPU-Zone-3 management-module 51.00 C normal 1/1-CPU-Zone-4 management-module 51.00 C normal 1/1-CPU-diode management-module 53.12 C normal 1/1-DDR management-module 45.25 C normal 1/1-Inlet-Air management-module 24.88 C norm.
For 4100i AOS-CX 10.17.1000, what does the show hostname command do?
The show hostname command shows the current host name. Syntax: show hostname.
For 4100i AOS-CX 10.17.1000, what is the syntax of the show hostname command?
Syntax: show hostname
For 4100i AOS-CX 10.17.1000, what does the show startup-config command do?

The show startup-config command shows the contents of the startup configuration. NOTE Switches in the factory-default configuration do not have a startup configuration to display. Display output in JSON format. json. Syntax: show startup-config [json].

For 4100i AOS-CX 10.17.1000, what does the show system resource-utilization command do?

The show system resource-utilization command shows the system resource utilization data. Public show system resource-utilization 492 Shows the resource utilization data for the entire switch. Showing system resource utilization data: switch# show system resource-utilization System Resources: Processes: 144 CPU usage(%): 10 CPU usage(% average over 1 minute): 11 CPU usage(% average over 5 minute): 15 Memory usage(%): 22 Open FD's: 1358 Storage 1: Endurance utilization = 10-20% (mmc-type-a), 0-10% (mmc-type-b), Health = normal Data written to various partitions since boot Nos: 5 MB Log: 1 MB Coredump: 23 MB. Syntax: show tech [basic | <FEATURE>] [local-file]
For 4100i AOS-CX 10.17.1000, what does the show usb command do?
The show usb command shows the USB port configuration and mount settings. Syntax: show usb.
For 4100i AOS-CX 10.17.1000, what is the syntax of the show usb command?
Syntax: show usb
For 4100i AOS-CX 10.17.1000, what does the top cpu command do?
The top cpu command shows CPU utilization information. Syntax: top cpu.
For 4100i AOS-CX 10.17.1000, what is the syntax of the top cpu command?
Syntax: top cpu
For 4100i AOS-CX 10.17.1000, what does the top memory command do?
The top memory command shows memory utilization information. Syntax: top memory.
