messages.content: For 4100i AOS-CX 10.17.1000, what is VSX?

VSX: d monitors (both enabled and disabled) compared to the maximum number supported on the switch. For example, 7/25 indicates that there are a total of 7 ou t of a supported maximum of 25. Clicking the link dis plays the Analytics Dashboard. Not applicable to the 6000 or 6100 Switch Series. Shows: Current firmware version, Primary version, an d Secondary version. Clicking the link displays the Fi rmware Update page. Shows: Most recent checkpoint and total number of checkpoints. Clicking the link displays the Config Mg mt page. Shows: Detected module name, Active, and Standby status information. Shows: New log entries over the last 15 seconds. Clic king the link displays the Log page. Shows: Current average CPU utilization per management module. Shows: Percent memory usage per management m odule. Shows: System Uptime, System Description, System Location, System Contact, Serial number, Base MAC, BIOS Version, Total number of available interfaces, a nd a pie chart for link status. Shows: Summary count of alerts. Clicking the link dis plays the Environmental page.
For 4100i AOS-CX 10.17.1000, what does the diag ospfv3 lsdb dump command do?

The diag ospfv3 lsdb dump command this command dumps the OSPFv3 AF protocol LSA information. Disables the OSPFv3 AF process. By default OSPFv3 AF process is enabled. This command does not remove the OSPFv3 AF configurations. Public disable 298. Syntax: diag ospfv3 lsdb dump.
For 4100i AOS-CX 10.17.1000, what is the syntax of the diag ospfv3 lsdb dump command?
Syntax: diag ospfv3 lsdb dump
For 4100i AOS-CX 10.17.1000, what does the router ospfv3-af command do?

The router ospfv3-af command creates the OSPFv3 AF process (if not created already) and enters the router OSPFv3 AF instance context. Optionally if specified, you can specify a named VRF, or the default VRF if the <vrf-name> is not specified. Maximum of eight OSPFv3 processes are allowed per VRF. The switch allows configuration of OSPFv2, OSPFv3, OSPFv3 IPv4 AF and OSPFv3 IPv6 AF. But at any point only 8 OSPF instances can run per address-family, 8 instances for IPv4 (OSPFv2 + OSPFv3 IPv4 AF) and 8 instances for IPv6 (OSPFv3 + OSPFv3 IPv6 AF). The no form of this command removes the OSPFv3 AF instance. If a VRF is specified, it removes the OSPF instance from the named VRF, or the default VRF if the <var-name> is not specified. Public router ospfv3-af 358 Specifies an OSPFv3 AF process ID. Length: 1 to 65535. Syntax: router ospfv3-af <PROCESS-ID> [vrf <VRF-NAME>].
For 4100i AOS-CX 10.17.1000, what is the syntax of the router ospfv3-af command?
Syntax: router ospfv3-af <PROCESS-ID> [vrf <VRF-NAME>]
For 4100i AOS-CX 10.17.1000, what is the syntax of the show ospfv3-af IPv4 command?
Syntax: show ospfv3-af [<PROCESS-ID>] IPv4 [all-vrfs | vrf <VRF-NAME>]
For 4100i AOS-CX 10.17.1000, what is the syntax of the show ospfv3-af IPv4 interface command?
Syntax: show ospfv3-af [<PROCESS-ID>] IPv4 interface [<INTERFACE-NAME>] | vlan
For 4100i AOS-CX 10.17.1000, what does the show ospfv3-af IPv4 neighbors command do?

The show ospfv3-af IPv4 neighbors command shows information about OSPFv3 AF neighbors. Public show ospfv3-af IPv4 neighbors 381 <PROCESS-ID> Specifies an OSPFv3 AF process ID to show OSPFv3 AF neigh bor information for the particular OSPFv3 AF process. Range: 1 to 65535. neighbors <NEIGHBOR-ID> Shows information about a particular neighbor, specified in IP v 4 format (A. B. C. D). interface <INTERFACE-NAME> Shows neighbor information only for the specified interface. vlan <VLAN-ID> Specifies the vlan interface name. lag <LAG-ID> Specifies the lag interface name. loopback <LOOPBACK-ID> Specifies the loopback interface name. tunnel <TUNNEL-ID> Specifies the tunnel interface name. detail summary all-vrfs Shows detailed information for the neighbors. Shows summary information for the neighbors. Shows neighbor information for all VRFs. Syntax: show ospfv3-af [<PROCESS-ID>] IPv4 neighbors [<NEIGHBOR-ID>].
For 4100i AOS-CX 10.17.1000, what is the syntax of the show ospfv3-af IPv4 neighbors command?
Syntax: show ospfv3-af [<PROCESS-ID>] IPv4 neighbors [<NEIGHBOR-ID>]
For 4100i AOS-CX 10.17.1000, what does the show ospfv3-af IPv4 routes command do?

The show ospfv3-af IPv4 routes command shows the OSPFv3 AF routing table information. <PROCESS-ID> <PREFIX/LENGTH> all-vrfs Specifies an OSPFv3 AF process ID that shows information fro m the OSPFv3 AF routing table for the particular OSPFv3 AF p rocess. Range: 1 to 65535. Specifies an IP address in IPv4 format (x.x.x.x), where x is a de cimal number from 0 to 255. Select to show OSPFv3 AF routing table information for all VR Fs. vrf <VRF-NAME> Specifies the name of a VRF. Default: default. Syntax: show ospfv3-af [<PROCESS-ID>] IPv4 routes [<PREFIX/LENGTH>].
For 4100i AOS-CX 10.17.1000, what is the syntax of the show ospfv3-af IPv4 routes command?
Syntax: show ospfv3-af [<PROCESS-ID>] IPv4 routes [<PREFIX/LENGTH>]
For 4100i AOS-CX 10.17.1000, what does the show ospfv3-af IPv4 statistics interface command do?

The show ospfv3-af IPv4 statistics interface command shows information about OSPFv3 AF enabled IPv4 interfaces. <PROCESS-ID> Specifies an OSPFv3 AF process ID optionally to show the OSP Fv3 AF enabled interfaces for the particular OSPFv3 AF proces s. Range: 1 to 65535. Selects to show information only for the specified OSPFv3 AF e nabled interface. Public show ospfv3-af IPv4 statistics interface 387 vlan <VLAN-ID> Specifies the vlan interface name. lag <LAG-ID> Specifies the lag interface name. loopback <LOOPBACK-ID> Specifies the loopback interface name. tunnel <TUNNEL-ID> Specifies the tunnel interface name. brief all-vrfs Include this parameter to display a brief overview of the followi ng OSPFv3 AF configuration information. Interface: OSPFv3 AF interface name. • • Area: OSPFv3 AF area ID. Syntax: show ospfv3-af [<PROCESS-ID>] IPv4 statistics interface [<INTERFACE-NAME>].
For 4100i AOS-CX 10.17.1000, what is the syntax of the show ospfv3-af IPv4 statistics interface command?
Syntax: show ospfv3-af [<PROCESS-ID>] IPv4 statistics interface [<INTERFACE-NAME>]
For 4100i AOS-CX 10.17.1000, what is the syntax of the show ospfv3-af IPv6 command?
Syntax: show ospfv3-af [<PROCESS-ID>] IPv6 [all-vrfs | vrf <VRF-NAME>]
For 4100i AOS-CX 10.17.1000, what does the show ospfv3-af IPv6 lsdb command do?

The show ospfv3-af IPv6 lsdb command shows the OSPFv3 AF link state database summary for different OSPF LSAs (Link State Advertisement). Use the parameters to get information for a particular LSA. Enter an OSPFv3 AF process ID to display general OSPF inform ation for a particular OSPF process. Range: 1 to 65535. <PROCESS-ID> Public show ospfv3-af IPv6 lsdb 401 adv-router {<ROUTER-ID>| self} Select to display link states for a particular advertising router. S pecify either a Router ID of the advertising router or specify sel f to show self-originated link states. area <AREA-ID> Select to display information filtered for the specified area in on e of the following formats. • OSPFv3 AF area identifier in IPv4 format (x.x.x.x), where x is a decimal number from 0 to 255. • OSPFv3 AF area identifier in decimal format. Syntax: show ospfv3-af [<process-id>] IPv6 lsdb.
For 4100i AOS-CX 10.17.1000, what is the syntax of the show ospfv3-af IPv6 lsdb command?
Syntax: show ospfv3-af [<process-id>] IPv6 lsdb
For 4100i AOS-CX 10.17.1000, what does the show ospfv3-af IPv6 neighbors command do?

The show ospfv3-af IPv6 neighbors command shows information about OSPFv3 AF neighbors. <PROCESS-ID> Specifies an OSPFv3 AF process ID to show OSPFv3 AF neigh bor information for the particular OSPFv3 AF process. Range: 1 to 65535. neighbors <NEIGHBOR-ID> Shows information about a particular neighbor, specified in IP v 4 format (A. B. C. D). Public show ospfv3-af IPv6 neighbors 408 interface <INTERFACE-NAME> Shows neighbor information only for the specified interface. detail summary Shows detailed information for the neighbors. Shows summary information for the neighbors. lag <LAG ID> Specifies lag interface name. loopback <LOOPBACK ID> Specifies the vlan interface name. all-vrfs vrf <VRF-NAME> Shows neighbor information for all VRFs. Specifies the name of a VRF. Default: default. Syntax: show ospfv3-af [<PROCESS-ID>] IPv6 neighbors [<NEIGHBOR-ID>].
For 4100i AOS-CX 10.17.1000, what is the syntax of the show ospfv3-af IPv6 neighbors command?
Syntax: show ospfv3-af [<PROCESS-ID>] IPv6 neighbors [<NEIGHBOR-ID>]
For 4100i AOS-CX 10.17.1000, what does the show ospfv3-af IPv6 routes command do?

The show ospfv3-af IPv6 routes command shows the OSPFv3 AF routing table information. Public show ospfv3-af IPv6 routes 411 <PROCESS-ID> <PREFIX/LENGTH> all-vrfs Specifies an OSPFv3 AF process ID that shows information fro m the OSPFv3 AF routing table for the particular OSPFv3 AF p rocess. Range: 1 to 65535. Specifies the IPv6 destination prefix showing information about a particular destination prefix. For example, 2010: bd9: : /32. Select to show OSPFv3 AF routing table information for all VR Fs. vrf <VRF-NAME> Specifies the name of a VRF. Default: default. Syntax: show ospfv3-af [<PROCESS-ID>] IPv6 routes [<PREFIX/LENGTH>].
For 4100i AOS-CX 10.17.1000, what is the syntax of the show ospfv3-af IPv6 routes command?
Syntax: show ospfv3-af [<PROCESS-ID>] IPv6 routes [<PREFIX/LENGTH>]
For 4100i AOS-CX 10.17.1000, what does the show ospfv3-af IPv6 statistics command do?

The show ospfv3-af IPv6 statistics command shows OSPFv3 AF statistics. <PROCESS-ID> all-vrfs Specifies an OSPFv3 AF process ID that shows information on t he OSPFv3 AF SPF statistics for the particular OSPFv3 AF proc ess. Range: 1 to 65535. Select to show OSPFv3 AF SPF statistics information for all VR Fs. vrf <VRF-NAME> Specifies the name of a VRF. Default: default. Syntax: show ospfv3-af IPv6 [<PROCESS-ID>] statistics.
For 4100i AOS-CX 10.17.1000, what is the syntax of the show ospfv3-af IPv6 statistics command?
Syntax: show ospfv3-af IPv6 [<PROCESS-ID>] statistics
For 4100i AOS-CX 10.17.1000, what does the show ospfv3-af IPv6 virtual-links command do?

The show ospfv3-af IPv6 virtual-links command displays the current state and parameters of the OSPFv3 AF virtual links. <PROCESS-ID> brief all-vrfs Enter an OSPFv3 AF process ID to display information on the O SPFv3 AF virtual links for the particular OSPFv3 AF process. Ra nge: 1 to 65535. Select to display brief overview information for the OSPFv3 AF virtual links. Select to display OSPFv3 AF virtual links information for all VR Fs. Public show ospfv3-af IPv6 virtual-links 419 Specifies the name of a VRF. Default: default. Syntax: show ospfv3-af [<PROCESS-ID>] IPv6 virtual-links [brief].
For 4100i AOS-CX 10.17.1000, what is the syntax of the show ospfv3-af IPv6 virtual-links command?
Syntax: show ospfv3-af [<PROCESS-ID>] IPv6 virtual-links [brief]
For 4100i AOS-CX 10.17.1000, what is the syntax of the show running-config all command?
Syntax: show running-config all
For 4100i AOS-CX 10.17.1000, what does the show tech ospfv3-af command do?

The show tech ospfv3-af command shows the output of the following OSPFv3 AF commands: • show ospfv3-af all vrfs • show ospfv3-af interface all-vrfs • show ospfv3-af neighbors detail all-vrfs • show ospfv3-af neighbors summary all-vrfs • show ospfv3-af route all-vrfs • show ospfv3-af statistics all-vrfs • show ospfv3-af statistics interface all-vrfs Public show tech ospfv3-af 441 • show ospfv3-af border-routers all-vrfs • show ospfv3-af lsdb all-vrfs database-summary • show ospfv3-af virtual-links all-vrfs Captures the command output into a local file. Optional. Syntax: show tech ospfv3-af [local-file].
For 4100i AOS-CX 10.17.1000, what is the syntax of the show tech ospfv3-af command?
Syntax: show tech ospfv3-af [local-file]
For 4100i AOS-CX 10.17.1000, what does the show ip ecmp command do?

The show ip ecmp command displays the Equal Cost Multipath (ECMP) configuration. Public Overview 457 Configures a primary or secondary IPv4 or IPv6 address for the VRRP group. To use secondary IP addresses in a VRRP group, you must first configure a primary IP address on the same group. A maximum of 16 IP addresses per IPv4 VRRP group and 8 IPv6 addresses per IPv6 VRRP group are supported. NOTE Do not configure an IPv4 VRRP group using addresses from the /30, /31, and /32 subnets of the interface IP address. 16 Virtual IP addresses per IPv4 VR and 8 Virtual IP addresses per IPv6 VR are supported. NOTE The total number of VIP s supported by a switch is: • 1024 VIP s for IPv4 VRs • 512 VIP s for IPv6 VRs The no form of this command deletes a primary or secondary IPv4 or IPv6 address from the VRRP group. Syntax: show ip ecmp.
For 4100i AOS-CX 10.17.1000, what is the syntax of the show ip ecmp command?
Syntax: show ip ecmp
For 4100i AOS-CX 10.17.1000, what does the router vrrp {enable disable} command do?

The router vrrp {enable disable} command enables or disables VRRP protocol globally. You must globally enable the VRRP feature for VRRP virtual router. no router vrrp enable disables VRRP protocol globally but does not remove all VRRP configurations. no router vrrp disable enables VRRP protocol globally. Syntax: router vrrp {enable | disable}.
For 4100i AOS-CX 10.17.1000, what is the syntax of the router vrrp {enable disable} command?
Syntax: router vrrp {enable | disable}
For 4100i AOS-CX 10.17.1000, what does the no router vrrp command do?

The no router vrrp command removes VRRP configuration and VRRP global protocol. If auto-confirm is enabled or VRRP is not configured on any interface, this command will not ask for user confirmation. Syntax: no router vrrp.
For 4100i AOS-CX 10.17.1000, what is the syntax of the no router vrrp command?
Syntax: no router vrrp
For 4100i AOS-CX 10.17.1000, what does the show track command do?

The show track command shows all or specific track object information. Displays brief information about all or specific track objects Displays information about a specified track object (1-128). Syntax: show track [brief | <OBJECT-ID>].
For 4100i AOS-CX 10.17.1000, what is the syntax of the show track command?
Syntax: show track [brief | <OBJECT-ID>]
For 4100i AOS-CX 10.17.1000, what does the show track brief command do?
The show track brief command shows brief information for all track objects. Syntax: show track brief.
For 4100i AOS-CX 10.17.1000, what is the syntax of the show track brief command?
Syntax: show track brief
For 4100i AOS-CX 10.17.1000, what does the apply policy command do?

The apply policy command applies a classifier policy containing a PBR action to an interface. A policy with PBR actions is only applicable to L3/routing interfaces. The no form of this command removes a classifier policy containing a PBR action from an interface. config-if Specifies name of the policy. • Layer 3 LAGs are not supported on 5420 or 6200 series switches. PBR policies cannot be applied to Layer 3 LAGs. Syntax: apply policy <POLICY-NAME> routed-in.
For 4100i AOS-CX 10.17.1000, what does the copy support-file feature command do?
The copy support-file feature command captures support logs to debug any IP Directed Broadcast issues. Syntax: copy support-file feature l3.
For 4100i AOS-CX 10.17.1000, what is the syntax of the copy support-file feature command?
Syntax: copy support-file feature l3
For 4100i AOS-CX 10.17.1000, what does the ip directed-broadcast command do?

The ip directed-broadcast command turns on IP Directed Broadcast for the specified interface. The no form of this command turns it off. This command is disabled by default. Syntax: ip directed-broadcast.
For 4100i AOS-CX 10.17.1000, what is the syntax of the ip directed-broadcast command?
Syntax: ip directed-broadcast
For 4100i AOS-CX 10.17.1000, what does the router rip command do?

The router rip command creates RIP process if not already created and enters the router rip <PROCESS-ID> context for the VRF mentioned. If no VRF is mentioned, a default is used. Only one RIP process is allowed per VRF. The no form of this command deletes the RIP instance for the VRF. If no VRF is mentioned the default is deleted. Specifies name of the RIP process ID. Range: 1-63. <PROCESS-ID> vrf <VRF-NAME> Specifies VRF name. Syntax: router rip <PROCESS-ID> [vrf <VRF-NAME>].
For 4100i AOS-CX 10.17.1000, what is the syntax of the router rip command?
Syntax: router rip <PROCESS-ID> [vrf <VRF-NAME>]
For 4100i AOS-CX 10.17.1000, what does the clear ip rip statistics command do?

The clear ip rip statistics command clears RIP event statistics. Specifies RIP process ID. Range: 1-63 Clears statistics for all VRFs. Selects VRF to clear statistics for. Specifies VRF name. <PROCESS-ID> all-vrfs vrf <VRF-NAME> Enables RIP process for RIP enabled IP address configured on interface. The no form of this command disables RIP process on interface. Disables RIP process for RIP enabled IP addresses configured on interface. The no form of this command enables RIP process on interface. Disables an interface from sending RIP packets for a specific IP address. The no form of this command enables interface for sending RIP packets for a specific IP address. Disables interface from receiving RIP packets for a specific IP address. Syntax: clear ip rip [<PROCESS-ID>] statistics [all-vrfs | vrf <VRF-NAME>].
For 4100i AOS-CX 10.17.1000, what is the syntax of the clear ip rip statistics command?
Syntax: clear ip rip [<PROCESS-ID>] statistics [all-vrfs | vrf <VRF-NAME>]
For 4100i AOS-CX 10.17.1000, what does the show capacities rip command do?
The show capacities rip command displays maximum number of RIP interfaces, routes and process. Syntax: show capacities rip.
For 4100i AOS-CX 10.17.1000, what is the syntax of the show capacities rip command?
Syntax: show capacities rip
For 4100i AOS-CX 10.17.1000, what does the show capacities-status rip command do?
The show capacities-status rip command displays number of RIP interfaces, routes and process configured in the system. Syntax: show capacities-status rip.
For 4100i AOS-CX 10.17.1000, what is the syntax of the show capacities-status rip command?
Syntax: show capacities-status rip
For 4100i AOS-CX 10.17.1000, what is the syntax of the show ip rip command?
Syntax: show ip rip [<PROCESS-ID>] [all-vrfs | vrf <VRF-NAME>]
For 4100i AOS-CX 10.17.1000, what does the router ripng command do?

The router ripng command creates RIPng process if not already created and enters the router ripng <PROCESS-ID> context for the VRF mentioned. If no VRF is mentioned, a default is used. Only one RIPng process is allowed per VRF. The no form of this command deletes the RIPng instance for the VRF. If no VRF is mentioned the default is deleted. Specifies name of the RIPng process ID. Range: <1-63> <PROCESS-ID> Public RIPng (IPv6) commands 599 Sets VRF name for RIPng process. VRF name for VRF. Syntax: router ripng <PROCESS-ID> [vrf <VRF-NAME>].
For 4100i AOS-CX 10.17.1000, what is the syntax of the router ripng command?
Syntax: router ripng <PROCESS-ID> [vrf <VRF-NAME>]
For 4100i AOS-CX 10.17.1000, what does the clear IPv6 ripng statistics command do?

The clear IPv6 ripng statistics command clears RIPng event statistics. Public RIPng clear commands 609 Specifies RIPng process ID. Range: 1-63 <PROCESS-ID> all-vrfs vrf <VRF-NAME> Clears statistics for all VRFs. Selects VRF to clear statistics for. Specifies VRF name. Enables RIPng process on interface. The no form of this command disables RIPng process on interface. Disables RIPng process on interface. The no form of this command enables RIPng process on interface. Disables interface from sending RIPng packets. An interface can send RIPng packets by default. The no form of this command enables interface to send RIPng packets, if disabled. Disables interface from receiving RIPng packets for all enabled IP addresses. An interface can receive RIPng packets by default. Syntax: clear IPv6 ripng [<PROCESS-ID>] statistics [all-vrfs | vrf <VRF-NAME>].
For 4100i AOS-CX 10.17.1000, what is the syntax of the clear IPv6 ripng statistics command?
Syntax: clear IPv6 ripng [<PROCESS-ID>] statistics [all-vrfs | vrf <VRF-NAME>]
For 4100i AOS-CX 10.17.1000, what does the show capacities ripng command do?
The show capacities ripng command displays the maximum number of RIPng interfaces, routes and process. Syntax: show capacities ripng.
For 4100i AOS-CX 10.17.1000, what is the syntax of the show capacities ripng command?
Syntax: show capacities ripng
For 4100i AOS-CX 10.17.1000, what does the show capacities-status ripng command do?
The show capacities-status ripng command displays number of RIPng interfaces, routes and process configured in the system. Syntax: show capacities-status ripng.
For 4100i AOS-CX 10.17.1000, what is the syntax of the show capacities-status ripng command?
Syntax: show capacities-status ripng
For 4100i AOS-CX 10.17.1000, how do you configure IRDP?

To configure IRDP, follow the documented procedure: 1. Enable IRDP on an interface with the command ip irdp 2. Set the maximum hold time with the command ip irdp holdtime 3. Set the maximum router advertisement interval with the command ip irdp maxadvertinterv 4. Set the minimum router advertisement interval with the command ip irdp minadvertinterv 5. Set the IRDP preference level with the command ip irdp preference 6. Review IRDP configuration settings with the command show ip irdp. Commands shown in the procedure include: interface vlan 2.
For 4100i AOS-CX 10.17.1000, how do you configure IPv6 RA?

To configure IPv6 RA, follow the documented procedure: 1. Enable transmission of IPv6 router advertisements with the command no IPv6 nd suppress-r 2. Optionally, configure IPv6 unicast address prefixes with the command IPv6 nd prefix 3. Optionally, configure support for DNS name resolution with the commands IPv6 nd ra dns ser.
For 4100i AOS-CX 10.17.1000, what does the IPv6 address autoconfig command do?

The IPv6 address autoconfig command enables the interface to automatically obtain an IPv6 address using router advertisement information and the EUI-64 identifier. Public IPv6 address autoconfig 33 The no form of this command disables address auto-configuration. NOTE • A maximum of 15 autoconfigured addresses are supported. • This command automatically creates an IPv6 link-local address on the interface. However, it does not add the IPv6 address link-loca l command to the running configuration. If you remove the IPv6 address, the link-local address is also removed. To maintain the link-local address, you must manually execute the IPv6 address link-local command. Syntax: IPv6 address autoconfig.
For 4100i AOS-CX 10.17.1000, what is the syntax of the IPv6 address autoconfig command?
Syntax: IPv6 address autoconfig
For 4100i AOS-CX 10.17.1000, what does the IPv6 address link-local command do?

The IPv6 address link-local command enables IPv6 on the current interface. If no address is specified, an IPv6 link-local address is auto-generated for the interface. If an address is specified, auto-configuration is disabled and the specified address/mask is assigned to the interface. To disable IPv6 link-local on the interface, remove IPv6 address link-local, IPv6 address <global-IPv6- address>, and IPv6 address autoconfig from the interface. NOTE This feature is not available on the management VRF. <IPv6-ADDR> <MASK> Specifies the IP address in IPv6 format (xxxx: xxxx: xxxx: xxxx: xxxx: xxxx: xxxx: xxxx), where x is a hexadecimal number from 0 to F. You can use two colons (: :) to represent consecutive zero s (but only once), remove leading zeros, and collapse a hextet o f four zeros to a single 0. Syntax: IPv6 address link-local [<IPv6-ADDR>/<MASK>].
For 4100i AOS-CX 10.17.1000, what is the syntax of the IPv6 address link-local command?
Syntax: IPv6 address link-local [<IPv6-ADDR>/<MASK>]
For 4100i AOS-CX 10.17.1000, what does the IPv6 nd cache-limit command do?

The IPv6 nd cache-limit command configures the limit on the number of neighbor entries in the ND cache. The no form of this command sets the cache limit to the default value. <CACHELIMIT> Specifies the neighbor cache entries limit. Range: 1-131072. Default: 131072. Public IPv6 nd cache-limit 36. Syntax: IPv6 nd cache-limit <CACHELIMIT>.
For 4100i AOS-CX 10.17.1000, what is the syntax of the IPv6 nd cache-limit command?
Syntax: IPv6 nd cache-limit <CACHELIMIT>
For 4100i AOS-CX 10.17.1000, what does the IPv6 nd dad attempts command do?

The IPv6 nd dad attempts command configures the number of neighbor solicitations to be sent when performing duplicate address detection (DAD) for a unicast address configured on an interface. If the active gateway is configured with the same IP as an SVI IP, then IPv6 DAD cannot be configured. The no form of this command sets the number of attempts to the default value. dad attempts <NUM-ATTEMPTS> Specifies the number of neighbor solicitations to send. Range: 0 -15. Default: 1. Public IPv6 nd dad attempts 37. Syntax: IPv6 nd dad attempts <NUM-ATTEMPTS>.
For 4100i AOS-CX 10.17.1000, what is the syntax of the IPv6 nd dad attempts command?
Syntax: IPv6 nd dad attempts <NUM-ATTEMPTS>
For 4100i AOS-CX 10.17.1000, what does the IPv6 nd hop-limit command do?

The IPv6 nd hop-limit command configures the hop limit to be sent in RAs. The no form of this command resets the hop limit to 0. This reset eliminates the hop limit from the RAs that originate on the interface, so the host determines the hop limit. hop-limit <HOPLIMIT> Specifies the hop limit. Range: 0-255. Default: 64. Public IPv6 nd hop-limit 38. Syntax: IPv6 nd hop-limit <HOPLIMIT>.
For 4100i AOS-CX 10.17.1000, what is the syntax of the IPv6 nd hop-limit command?
Syntax: IPv6 nd hop-limit <HOPLIMIT>
For 4100i AOS-CX 10.17.1000, what does the IPv6 nd mtu command do?

The IPv6 nd mtu command configures the MTU size to be sent in the RA messages. The no form of this command sets hop limit to the default value. <MTU-VALUE> Specifies the MTU size. Range: 1280-65535 bytes. Default: 1 500 bytes. Public IPv6 nd mtu 39. Syntax: IPv6 nd mtu <MTU-VALUE>.
For 4100i AOS-CX 10.17.1000, what is the syntax of the IPv6 nd mtu command?
Syntax: IPv6 nd mtu <MTU-VALUE>
For 4100i AOS-CX 10.17.1000, what does the IPv6 nd ns-interval command do?

The IPv6 nd ns-interval command configures the ND time in milliseconds between DAD neighbor solicitations sent for an unresolved destination. Increase the ns-interval time if the network is slow or if there are persistent retry failures. If the active gateway is configured with the same IP as an SVI IP, then IPv6 DAD cannot be configured The no form of this command sets the ns-interval to the default value. Specifies the neighbor solicitation interval. Range: 1000-3600 000 milliseconds. Default: 1000 milliseconds. <TIME> Public IPv6 nd ns-interval 40. Syntax: IPv6 nd ns-interval <TIME>.
For 4100i AOS-CX 10.17.1000, what is the syntax of the IPv6 nd ns-interval command?
Syntax: IPv6 nd ns-interval <TIME>
For 4100i AOS-CX 10.17.1000, what does the IPv6 nd prefix command do?

The IPv6 nd prefix command specifies prefixes for the routing switch to include in RAs transmitted on the interface. IPv6 hosts use the prefixes in RAs to autoconfigure themselves with global unicast addresses. The autoconfigured address of a host is composed of the advertised prefix and the interface identifier in the current link-local address of the host. Public IPv6 nd prefix 41 By default, advertise, autoconfig, and onlink are set. The no form of this command removes the configuration on the interface. Specifies the IPv6 prefix to advertise in RA. Format: X: X: : X: X/M <IPv6-ADDR>/<PREFIX-LEN> default Specifies apply configuration to all on-link prefixes that are n ot individually set by the IPv6 ra prefix <IPv6-ADDR>/<PRE FIX-LEN> command. Syntax: IPv6 nd prefix <IPv6-ADDR>/<PREFIX-LEN>.
For 4100i AOS-CX 10.17.1000, what is the syntax of the IPv6 nd prefix command?
Syntax: IPv6 nd prefix <IPv6-ADDR>/<PREFIX-LEN>
For 4100i AOS-CX 10.17.1000, what does the IPv6 nd ra dns search-list command do?

The IPv6 nd ra dns search-list command configures the DNS Search List (DNSSL) to include in Router Advertisements (RAs) transmitted on the interface. The no form of this command removes the DNS Search List from the RAs transmitted on the interface. Public IPv6 nd ra dns search-list 43 Specifies the domain names for DNS queries. <DOMAIN-NAME> lifetime <TIME>. Syntax: IPv6 nd ra dns search-list <DOMAIN-NAME> [lifetime <TIME>].
For 4100i AOS-CX 10.17.1000, what is the syntax of the IPv6 nd ra dns search-list command?
Syntax: IPv6 nd ra dns search-list <DOMAIN-NAME> [lifetime <TIME>]
For 4100i AOS-CX 10.17.1000, what does the IPv6 nd ra dns server command do?

The IPv6 nd ra dns server command configures the IPv6 address of a preferred Recursive DNS Server (RDNSS) to be included in Router Advertisements (RAs) transmitted on the interface. The no form of this command removes the configured DNS server from the RAs transmitted on the interface. <IPv6-ADDR> Specifies the RDNSS address in IPv6 format (xxxx: xxxx: xxxx: x xxx: xxxx: xxxx: xxxx: xxxx), where x is a hexadecimal number f rom 0 to F. You can use two colons (: :) to represent consecutive zeros (but only once), remove leading zeros, and collapse a hex tet of four zeros to a single 0. For example, this address 2222: 0 000: 3333: 0000: 0000: 0000: 4444: 0055 becomes 2222: 0: 333 3: : 4444: 55. lifetime <TIME> Specifies IPv6 DNS server lifetime in seconds. Range: 4-4294 967295 seconds. Default: 1800 seconds. Syntax: IPv6 nd ra dns server <IPv6-ADDR> [lifetime <TIME>].
For 4100i AOS-CX 10.17.1000, what is the syntax of the IPv6 nd ra dns server command?
Syntax: IPv6 nd ra dns server <IPv6-ADDR> [lifetime <TIME>]
For 4100i AOS-CX 10.17.1000, what does the IPv6 nd ra lifetime command do?

The IPv6 nd ra lifetime command configures the lifetime, in seconds, for the routing switch to be used as a default router by hosts on the current interface. The no form of this command sets lifetime to the default of 1800 seconds. Specifies lifetime in seconds of a default router. A setting of 0 fo r default router lifetime in an RA indicates that the routing switc h is not a default router on the interface. Range: 0-9000 seco nds. Default: 1800 seconds. <TIME>. Syntax: IPv6 nd ra lifetime <TIME>.
For 4100i AOS-CX 10.17.1000, what is the syntax of the IPv6 nd ra lifetime command?
Syntax: IPv6 nd ra lifetime <TIME>
For 4100i AOS-CX 10.17.1000, what does the IPv6 nd ra min-interval command do?

The IPv6 nd ra min-interval command configures the minimum interval between transmissions of IPv6 RAs on the interface. The interval between RA transmissions on an interface is a random value that changes every time an RA is sent. The interval is calculated to be a value between the current max-interval and min-interval settings. The no form of this command returns the setting to its default, provided the default value is less than the current max-interval setting. Specifies a minimum advertisement time in seconds. Range: 3 -1350. Default: 200 seconds. <TIME>. Syntax: IPv6 nd ra min-interval <TIME>.
For 4100i AOS-CX 10.17.1000, what is the syntax of the IPv6 nd ra min-interval command?
Syntax: IPv6 nd ra min-interval <TIME>
For 4100i AOS-CX 10.17.1000, what does the IPv6 nd ra other-config-flag command do?

The IPv6 nd ra other-config-flag command controls the O-bit in RAs the router transmits on the current interface; but is ignored unless the M-bit is disabled in RAs. Configure to set the O-bit in RA messages for host to obtain network parameters through DHCPv6. The other-config-flag is disabled by default. For more information on configuring the M-bit, see IPv6 nd ra managed-config-flag. The no form of this command turns off (disables) the setting for this command in RAs. Syntax: IPv6 nd ra other-config-flag.
For 4100i AOS-CX 10.17.1000, what is the syntax of the IPv6 nd ra other-config-flag command?
Syntax: IPv6 nd ra other-config-flag
For 4100i AOS-CX 10.17.1000, what does the IPv6 nd ra reachable-time command do?

The IPv6 nd ra reachable-time command sets the amount of time that the interface considers a device to be reachable after receiving a reachability confirmation from the device. The no form of this command sets the reachable time to the default value of 0. (no limit). Specifies the reachable time in milliseconds. Range: 1000-36 00000. Default: 0 (no limit). <TIME>. Syntax: IPv6 nd ra reachable-time <TIME>.
For 4100i AOS-CX 10.17.1000, what is the syntax of the IPv6 nd ra reachable-time command?
Syntax: IPv6 nd ra reachable-time <TIME>
For 4100i AOS-CX 10.17.1000, what does the IPv6 nd ra retrans-timer command do?

The IPv6 nd ra retrans-timer command configures the period (retransmit timer) between ND solicitations sent by a host for an unresolved destination, or between DAD neighbor solicitation requests. By default, hosts on the interface use their own locally configured NS-interval settings instead of using the value received in the RAs. Increase this timer when neighbor solicitation retries or failures are occur, or in a "slow" (WAN) network. The no form of this command sets the value to the default of 0. <TIME> Specifies the retransmit timer value in milliseconds. Range: 0 - 4294967295 milliseconds. Default: 0 (Use locally configured N S-interval). Public IPv6 nd ra retrans-timer 53. Syntax: IPv6 nd ra retrans-timer <TIME>.
For 4100i AOS-CX 10.17.1000, what is the syntax of the IPv6 nd ra retrans-timer command?
Syntax: IPv6 nd ra retrans-timer <TIME>
For 4100i AOS-CX 10.17.1000, what does the guide say about Interface counters polling?

Interface counters polling: • Agent IP configuration for IPv4 and IPv6 • Header size configuration • Max datagram size configuration • Ingress sampling for all forwarded traffic (L2, L3) • Enable/Disable sFlow per interface • Support for three remote collectors • An out-of-band collector can be defined on the management VRF • A collector can be defined on the default and non-default VRF • Sampling on Ethernet and LAG interfaces • High availability support (sampling continues to work after switch-over) Public sFlow agent 71 • Source IP support (setting source IP for sFlow datagrams sent to a remote collector) NOTE For the 4100i, 6000, and 6100 switch series, collector can be defined only on the default VRF. Also, there is no sampling of egress traffic. Limitations • Sampling rate cannot be set per interface (global only) • sFlow is not configurable through SNMP.
For 4100i AOS-CX 10.17.1000, what does the clear sflow statistics command do?

The clear sflow statistics command this command clears the sFlow sample statistics counter to 0 either globally or for a specific interface. Specifies all interfaces on the switch. interface <INTERFACE-NAME> Specifies the name of an interface on the switch. Syntax: clear sflow statistics {global | interface <INTERFACE-NAME>}.
For 4100i AOS-CX 10.17.1000, what is the syntax of the clear sflow statistics command?
Syntax: clear sflow statistics {global | interface <INTERFACE-NAME>}
For 4100i AOS-CX 10.17.1000, what does the sflow collector <IP-ADDR> [port <PORT>] [vrf <VRF>] command do?

The sflow collector <IP-ADDR> [port <PORT>] [vrf <VRF>] command defines a collector to which the sFlow agent sends data. Up to three collectors can be defined. At least one collector should be defined, and it must be reachable from the switch for sFlow to work. collector <IP-ADDR> port <PORT> vrf <VRF>. Syntax: sflow collector <IP-ADDR> [port <PORT>] [vrf <VRF>].
For 4100i AOS-CX 10.17.1000, what is the syntax of the sflow collector <IP-ADDR> [port <PORT>] [vrf <VRF>] command?
Syntax: sflow collector <IP-ADDR> [port <PORT>] [vrf <VRF>]
For 4100i AOS-CX 10.17.1000, what does the sflow disable command do?
The sflow disable command disables the sFlow agent, but retains any existing sFlow configuration settings. The settings become active if the sFlow agent is re-enabled. Syntax: sflow disable.
For 4100i AOS-CX 10.17.1000, what is the syntax of the sflow disable command?
Syntax: sflow disable
For 4100i AOS-CX 10.17.1000, what does the sflow sampling <RATE> command do?

The sflow sampling <RATE> command defines the global sampling rate for sFlow in number of packets. The default sampling rate is 4096, which means that one in every 4096 packets is sampled. A warning message is displayed when the sampling rate is set to less than 4096 and proceeds only after user confirmation. The no form of this command sets the sampling rate to the default value of 4096. sampling <RATE> Specifies the sampling rate. Range: 1 to 16777215. Default: 40 96. Syntax: sflow sampling <RATE>.
For 4100i AOS-CX 10.17.1000, what is the syntax of the sflow sampling <RATE> command?
Syntax: sflow sampling <RATE>
For 4100i AOS-CX 10.17.1000, what does the guide say about Configuration task list?

Configuration task list: Public DHCP 92 Considerations and best practices Use case DHCP client commands Protocol and feature details DHCP Client is supported on IPv4. The IP DHCP assignment with HPE Aruba Networking AOS-CX switch is to allow the following: • IP switch assignment. • Managing the initial IP access to the switch. • Prepare for Zero Touch Provisioning (ZTP) with HPE Aruba Networking Central. NOTE Zero Touch Provisioning (ZTP) is only supported for IPv4 in HPE Aruba Networking Central • Prepare for Zero Touch Provisioning (ZTP) by using DHCP options along with a TFTP server. Supported platform and standards The following table list the supported VLANs and ports for the AOS-CX Switch.
For 4100i AOS-CX 10.17.1000, what does the no ip dhcp preferred-vlan vlan <VLAN-ID> [vrf <VRF-NAME>] command do?

The no ip dhcp preferred-vlan vlan <VLAN-ID> [vrf <VRF-NAME>] command specifies the preferred VLAN for DHCP client options, including DNS, NTP, and Default Gateway. The preferred VLAN is the VLAN used to select DHCP client options within a specified VRF when multiple VLANs have DHCP client enabled in the same VRF. If no VRF is specified, the configuration applies to the default VRF. In cases where the preferred VLAN is not explicitly configured, the lowest VLAN with DHCP client enabled will be automatically selected. The no form of the command removes the preferred VLAN configuration and restores the default behavior. VLAN ID to set as preferred VLAN. VLAN identifier range: 1 to 4094. Public ip dhcp preferred-vlan 102 Specifies the VRF in which the preferred VLANshould be confi gured.
For 4100i AOS-CX 10.17.1000, what does the dhcp-relay hop-count-increment command do?

The dhcp-relay hop-count-increment command enables the DHCP relay hop count increment feature, which causes the DHCP relay agent to increment the hop count in all relayed DHCP packets. Hop count is enabled by default. The no form of this command disables the hop count increment feature. Syntax: dhcp-relay hop-count-increment.
For 4100i AOS-CX 10.17.1000, what is the syntax of the dhcp-relay hop-count-increment command?
Syntax: dhcp-relay hop-count-increment

section: 4. Which VLAN is assigned a DHCP address?

messages.content: For 4100i AOS-CX 10.17.1000, what does the guide say about 4. Which VLAN is assigned a DHCP address? ?

4. Which VLAN is assigned a DHCP address? : The factory default VLAN to which the DHCP address is assigned is VLAN1. Users can configure the VLAN for DHCP allocation on platforms supporting multiple VLAN configurations. Only a single VLAN can support DHCP assignments. 5. Can DHCP assignments be disabled in an HPE Aruba Networking AOS-CX switch? Use the no ip dhcp command on the required VLAN interface or management VRF port you can disable the DHCP assignments in an HPE Aruba Networking AOS-CX switch. A static configuration option is available on both ports. 6. Can IP DHCP switch assignment be carried out on a Routed only Port (RoP)? No, RoP is not supported. Only VLAN and OOBM ports can be allocated an address with DHCP. 7. Can an HPE Aruba Networking AOS-CX switch support DHCP IPv6 Allocation? Yes, an HPE Aruba Networking AOS-CX switch can support DHCP IPv6 allocation. However, for Zero Touch Provisioning (ZTP), IPv4 is required. 8.

topic: 4. Which VLAN is assigned a DHCP address?

messages.content: For 4100i AOS-CX 10.17.1000, what does the guide say about snooping on required VLANs?

snooping on required VLANs: vlan 111 description uplink dhcpv4-snooping vlan 120 description User dhcpv4-snooping interface 1/1/1 no shutdown description User vlan access 120... interface 1/1/15 no shutdown description User vlan access 120 interface 1/1/51 <--Unplinks on 1/1/51 and 52 trusted no shutdown vlan trunk native 1 vlan trunk allowed 110 dhcpv4-snooping trust interface 1/1/52 vlan trunk native 1 vlan trunk allowed 111 dhcpv4-snooping trust interface vlan 110 description uplink ip address 172.16.69.1/30 interface vlan 111 description uplink ip address 172.16.71.1/30 interface vlan 120 description User ip address 172.16.10.1/254 ip helper-address 10.19.69.15 ip helper-address 10.19.71.15 Public DHCPv4 Snooping Use case 149... ip route 0.0.0.0/0 172.16.69.2 distance 10 ip route 0.0.0.0/0 172.16.71.2 distance 20... Using the show dhcpv4-snooping command, you can see some of the following DHCPv4 information:
For 4100i AOS-CX 10.17.1000, what does the clear dhcp-snooping statistics command do?
The clear dhcp-snooping statistics command clears all DHCP snooping statistics. Syntax: clear dhcp-snooping statistics.
For 4100i AOS-CX 10.17.1000, what is the syntax of the clear dhcp-snooping statistics command?
Syntax: clear dhcp-snooping statistics
For 4100i AOS-CX 10.17.1000, what is the syntax of the dhcp-snooping command?
Syntax: dhcp-snooping
For 4100i AOS-CX 10.17.1000, what does the dhcp-snooping allow-overwrite-binding command do?

The dhcp-snooping allow-overwrite-binding command allows binding to be overwritten for the same IP address. When enabled, and a DHCP server offers a host an IP address that is already bound to an existing host in the binding table, the existing binding is overwritten Public dhcp-snooping allow-overwrite-binding 156 for the new host if the new host is successfully able to acquire the same IP address. This overwriting is disabled by default, causing the DHCP server offers to be dropped. The no form of the command disables DHCP snooping overwrite binding. Syntax: dhcp-snooping allow-overwrite-binding.
For 4100i AOS-CX 10.17.1000, what is the syntax of the dhcp-snooping allow-overwrite-binding command?
Syntax: dhcp-snooping allow-overwrite-binding
For 4100i AOS-CX 10.17.1000, what does the dhcp-snooping authorized-server <IP-ADDR> [vrf <VRF-NAME>] command do?

The dhcp-snooping authorized-server <IP-ADDR> [vrf <VRF-NAME>] command adds an authorized (trusted) DHCP server to a list of authorized servers for use by DHCP snooping. This command can be issued multiple times, adding a maximum of 20 authorized servers per VRF. By default, with an empty list of authorized servers, all DHCP servers are considered to be trusted for DHCP snooping purposes. NOTE The mgmt VRF cannot be used with this command. The no form of this command deletes the specified DHCP server from the authorized list. Specifies the IP address of the trusted DHCP server. <IP-ADDR> vrf <VRF-NAME> Specifies the VRF name. Syntax: dhcp-snooping authorized-server <IP-ADDR> [vrf <VRF-NAME>].
For 4100i AOS-CX 10.17.1000, what is the syntax of the dhcp-snooping authorized-server <IP-ADDR> [vrf <VRF-NAME>] command?
Syntax: dhcp-snooping authorized-server <IP-ADDR> [vrf <VRF-NAME>]
For 4100i AOS-CX 10.17.1000, what does the dhcp-snooping event-log client command do?

The dhcp-snooping event-log client command this command enables or disables dhcp-snooping client level event logs that help with client telemetry on a remote management station such as HPE Aruba Networking Central. By default, client level event logs are disabled. The no form of this command disables client-level event logs for DHCP snooping after they are enabled. View these logged DHCP snooping events by issuing the command show events -c dhcp-snooping. NOTE For additional information on DHCP-related event logging, please refer to the Event Log Message Reference Guide. Syntax: dhcp-snooping event-log client.
For 4100i AOS-CX 10.17.1000, what is the syntax of the dhcp-snooping event-log client command?
Syntax: dhcp-snooping event-log client
For 4100i AOS-CX 10.17.1000, what does the dhcp-snooping external-storage volume <VOL-NAME> file <FILE-NAME> command do?

The dhcp-snooping external-storage volume <VOL-NAME> file <FILE-NAME> command configures external storage to be used for backing up IP bindings (used by DHCP snooping) to a file. When configured, the switch stores all the IP bindings in an external storage file so that they are retained after the Public dhcp-snooping external-storage 160 switch restarts. When the switch restarts, it reads the IP bindings from the configured external storage file to populate its local cache. NOTE When both external storage and flash storage are configured to store DHCP snooping IP bindings, the external storage takes priority, and is used exclusively until it becomes unconfigured, at which time flash storage (if configured) is used. Later, if external storage is configured again, flash storage stops and external storage resumes. Syntax: dhcp-snooping external-storage volume <VOL-NAME> file <FILE-NAME>.
For 4100i AOS-CX 10.17.1000, what is the syntax of the dhcp-snooping external-storage volume <VOL-NAME> file <FILE-NAME> command?
Syntax: dhcp-snooping external-storage volume <VOL-NAME> file <FILE-NAME>
For 4100i AOS-CX 10.17.1000, what does the dhcp-snooping flash-storage [delay <DELAY>] command do?

The dhcp-snooping flash-storage [delay <DELAY>] command configures switch flash storage to be used for backing up client IP bindings (used by DHCP snooping). When flash storage is configured (and external storage is not already configured for this purpose), the switch stores the IP bindings in switch flash storage. When the switch restarts, it reads the IP bindings from the switch flash storage to populate its local cache. Writing the IP bindings to flash storage only occurs after the configured delay and if there has been a change in client IP bindings. Writing is skipped when client IP bindings have not changed since the previous write. Public dhcp-snooping flash-storage 162 Omitting delay <DELAY> sets the default delay of 900 seconds. Syntax: dhcp-snooping flash-storage [delay <DELAY>].
For 4100i AOS-CX 10.17.1000, what is the syntax of the dhcp-snooping flash-storage [delay <DELAY>] command?
Syntax: dhcp-snooping flash-storage [delay <DELAY>]
For 4100i AOS-CX 10.17.1000, what does the dhcp-snooping max-bindings <MAX-BINDINGS> command do?

The dhcp-snooping max-bindings <MAX-BINDINGS> command sets the maximum number of DHCP bindings allowed on the selected interface. For all interfaces on which this command is not run, the default max binding is the maximum value of the range. The no form of the command reverts max bindings for the selected interface to its default. Specifies the maximum number of DHCP bindings. Range: 1 to 256. <MAX-BINDINGS>. Syntax: dhcp-snooping max-bindings <MAX-BINDINGS>.
For 4100i AOS-CX 10.17.1000, what is the syntax of the dhcp-snooping max-bindings <MAX-BINDINGS> command?
Syntax: dhcp-snooping max-bindings <MAX-BINDINGS>
For 4100i AOS-CX 10.17.1000, what does the dhcp-snooping static-attributes command do?

The dhcp-snooping static-attributes command enables storage of static attributes provided to the DHCP client by DHCP server during DHCP packet exchange. Disabled by default. When enabled, the following attributes are stored in OVSDB along with the client IP binding entry: 1. Name server IP addresses: DNS server IP s provided by the DHCP server to the client. Maximum: 3 per client. 2. Default gateway IP address: Router IP addresses provided by DHCP server to the client. Maximum: 3 per client. 3. Server IP address: IP address of the DHCP server that leased the IP to the client. Public dhcp-snooping static-attributes 167 The no form of the command disables storing of client static attributes. After disabling, existing client static attributes will be flushed. Syntax: dhcp-snooping static-attributes.
For 4100i AOS-CX 10.17.1000, what is the syntax of the dhcp-snooping static-attributes command?
Syntax: dhcp-snooping static-attributes
For 4100i AOS-CX 10.17.1000, what does the dhcp-snooping trust command do?

The dhcp-snooping trust command enables DHCP snooping trust on the selected port. Only server packets received on trusted ports are forwarded. All the ports are untrusted by default. Public dhcp-snooping trust 168 The no form of the command disables DHCP snooping trust on the selected port. Syntax: dhcp-snooping trust.
For 4100i AOS-CX 10.17.1000, what is the syntax of the dhcp-snooping trust command?
Syntax: dhcp-snooping trust
For 4100i AOS-CX 10.17.1000, what does the dhcp-snooping verify mac command do?

The dhcp-snooping verify mac command this command enables verification of the hardware address field in DHCP client packets. When enabled, the DHCP client hardware address field and the source MAC address must be the same for packets received on untrusted ports or else the packet is dropped. This DHCP snooping MAC verification is enabled by default. The no form of the command disables DHCP snooping MAC verification. Syntax: dhcp-snooping verify mac.
For 4100i AOS-CX 10.17.1000, what is the syntax of the dhcp-snooping verify mac command?
Syntax: dhcp-snooping verify mac
For 4100i AOS-CX 10.17.1000, what does the clear dhcpv6-snooping statistics command do?
The clear dhcpv6-snooping statistics command clears all DHCPv6 snooping statistics. Syntax: clear dhcpv6-snooping statistics.
For 4100i AOS-CX 10.17.1000, what is the syntax of the clear dhcpv6-snooping statistics command?
Syntax: clear dhcpv6-snooping statistics
For 4100i AOS-CX 10.17.1000, what is the syntax of the dhcpv6-snooping command?
Syntax: dhcpv6-snooping
For 4100i AOS-CX 10.17.1000, what does the dhcpv6-snooping authorized-server <IPv6-ADDR> [vrf <VRF-NAME>] command do?

The dhcpv6-snooping authorized-server <IPv6-ADDR> [vrf <VRF-NAME>] command adds an authorized (trusted) DHCPv6 server to a list of authorized servers for use by DHCPv6 snooping. This command can be issued multiple times, adding a maximum of 20 authorized servers per VRF. By default, with an empty list of authorized servers, all DHCPv6 servers are considered to be trusted for DHCPv6 snooping purposes. NOTE The mgmt VRF cannot be used with this command. NOTE Configure the link local IPv6 address instead of global IPv6 address of the DHCPv6 server as the authorized-server. For example: switch(config)# dhcpv6-snooping authorized-server fe80: : 2ca4: fa40: d4cd: bc2f The no form of this command deletes the specified DHCPv6 server from the authorized list. Specifies the IPv6 address of the trusted DHCPv6 server. <IPv6-ADDR> vrf <VRF-NAME> Specifies the VRF name. Syntax: dhcpv6-snooping authorized-server <IPv6-ADDR> [vrf <VRF-NAME>].
For 4100i AOS-CX 10.17.1000, what is the syntax of the dhcpv6-snooping authorized-server <IPv6-ADDR> [vrf <VRF-NAME>] command?
Syntax: dhcpv6-snooping authorized-server <IPv6-ADDR> [vrf <VRF-NAME>]
For 4100i AOS-CX 10.17.1000, what does the dhcpv6-snooping event-log client command do?

The dhcpv6-snooping event-log client command this command enables or disables DHCPv6 snooping client level event logs that help with client telemetry on a remote management station such as HPE Aruba Networking Central. By default, client level event logs are disabled. The no form of this command disables client-level event logs for DHCPv6 snooping after they are enabled. View these logged DHCPv6 snooping events by issuing the command show events -c dhcpv6-snooping. NOTE For additional information on DHCP-related event logging, please refer to the Event Log Message Reference Guide. Public dhcpv6-snooping event-log client 182. Syntax: dhcpv6-snooping event-log client.
For 4100i AOS-CX 10.17.1000, what is the syntax of the dhcpv6-snooping event-log client command?
Syntax: dhcpv6-snooping event-log client
For 4100i AOS-CX 10.17.1000, what does the dhcpv6-snooping external-storage volume <VOL-NAME> file <FILE-NAME> command do?

The dhcpv6-snooping external-storage volume <VOL-NAME> file <FILE-NAME> command configures external storage to be used for backing up IPv6 bindings (used by DHCPv6 snooping) to a file. When configured, the switch stores all the IP bindings in an external storage file so that they are retained Public dhcpv6-snooping external-storage 183 after the switch restarts. When the switch restarts, it reads the IPv6 bindings from the configured external storage file to populate its local cache. NOTE When both external storage and flash storage are configured to store DHCP snooping IP bindings, the external storage takes priority, and is used exclusively until it becomes unconfigured, at which time flash storage (if configured) is used. Later, if external storage is configured again, flash storage stops and external storage resumes. Syntax: dhcpv6-snooping external-storage volume <VOL-NAME> file <FILE-NAME>.
For 4100i AOS-CX 10.17.1000, what is the syntax of the dhcpv6-snooping external-storage volume <VOL-NAME> file <FILE-NAME> command?
Syntax: dhcpv6-snooping external-storage volume <VOL-NAME> file <FILE-NAME>
For 4100i AOS-CX 10.17.1000, what does the dhcpv6-snooping flash-storage [delay <DELAY>] command do?

The dhcpv6-snooping flash-storage [delay <DELAY>] command configures switch flash storage to be used for backing up client IP bindings (used by DHCPv6 snooping). When flash storage is configured (and external storage is not already configured for this purpose), the switch stores the IP bindings in switch flash storage. When the switch restarts, it reads the IP bindings from the switch flash storage to populate its local cache. Writing the IP bindings to flash storage only occurs after the configured delay and if there has been a change in client IP bindings. Writing is skipped when client IP bindings have not changed since the previous write. Public dhcpv6-snooping flash-storage 185 Omitting delay <DELAY> sets the default delay of 900 seconds. Syntax: dhcpv6-snooping flash-storage [delay <DELAY>].
For 4100i AOS-CX 10.17.1000, what is the syntax of the dhcpv6-snooping flash-storage [delay <DELAY>] command?
Syntax: dhcpv6-snooping flash-storage [delay <DELAY>]
For 4100i AOS-CX 10.17.1000, what does the dhcpv6-snooping max-bindings <MAX-BINDINGS> command do?

The dhcpv6-snooping max-bindings <MAX-BINDINGS> command sets the maximum number of DHCPv6 bindings allowed on the selected interface. For all interfaces on which this command is not run, the default max binding is the maximum value of the range. The no form of the command reverts max bindings for the selected interface to its default. Specifies the maximum number of DHCP bindings. Range: 1 to 256. <MAX-BINDINGS>. Syntax: dhcpv6-snooping max-bindings <MAX-BINDINGS>.
For 4100i AOS-CX 10.17.1000, what is the syntax of the dhcpv6-snooping max-bindings <MAX-BINDINGS> command?
Syntax: dhcpv6-snooping max-bindings <MAX-BINDINGS>
For 4100i AOS-CX 10.17.1000, what does the dhcpv6-snooping trust command do?

The dhcpv6-snooping trust command enables DHCPv6 snooping trust on the selected interface. Only server packets received on trusted interfaces are forwarded. All the interfaces are untrusted by default. The no form of the command disables DHCPv6 snooping trust on the selected interface. config-if. Syntax: dhcpv6-snooping trust.
For 4100i AOS-CX 10.17.1000, what is the syntax of the dhcpv6-snooping trust command?
Syntax: dhcpv6-snooping trust

section: 5. Server and client are connected to same the VLAN

messages.content: For 4100i AOS-CX 10.17.1000, what does the guide say about 5. Server and client are connected to same the VLAN?

5. Server and client are connected to same the VLAN: a. Check if the server is reachable on the same VLAN as the client. b. If the server is on a different L3 network, configure DHCP-Relay to forward the packets between the client and server. 6. Authorized server configurations-When configured, the server assigning IP address should be one of the IP addresses from the authorized server list. Public.

topic: 5. Server and client are connected to same the VLAN

messages.content: For 4100i AOS-CX 10.17.1000, what does the no IPv4 source-binding <VLAN-ID> command do?

The no IPv4 source-binding <VLAN-ID> command adds static IPv4 client source binding information to the switch IP binding database. Although DHCPv4 snooping is often used to dynamically populate the binding database, this command is available for manually adding entries to the switch IP binding database. NOTE Statically configured IP binding information supersedes any dynamically collected binding information for the same client. Public IPv4 source lockdown commands 200 The no form of this command removes the specified binding that was statically configured with the IPv4 source-binding command. The no form has no effect on bindings that were dynamically configured with DHCPv4 snooping. Specifies the ID of an existing VLAN on which the client is conn ected. Range: 1 to 4094.
For 4100i AOS-CX 10.17.1000, what does the IPv4 source-lockdown command do?

The IPv4 source-lockdown command enables IPv4 source lockdown for all VLANs on the selected interface (port). The no form of this command disables IPv4 source lockdown for the selected interface (port). CAUTION This configuration will disable flow tracking statistics collection. Syntax: IPv4 source-lockdown.
For 4100i AOS-CX 10.17.1000, what is the syntax of the IPv4 source-lockdown command?
Syntax: IPv4 source-lockdown
For 4100i AOS-CX 10.17.1000, what does the show IPv4 source-binding command do?
The show IPv4 source-binding command shows all IPv4 static source binding information irrespective of source lockdown configuration. Syntax: show IPv4 source-binding.
For 4100i AOS-CX 10.17.1000, what is the syntax of the show IPv4 source-binding command?
Syntax: show IPv4 source-binding
For 4100i AOS-CX 10.17.1000, what does the guide say about VLAN Id: 2000?

VLAN Id: 2000: MAC address: 00: 50: 56: 96: e4: cf IP Address: 192.168.142.113 Time Remaining: static Lockdown Status: Yes Hardware Status: Yes Hardware Error Reason: -- Showing the detailed binding record and related information for interface 1/1/2 (identified in this example command by the VLAN): switch# show IPv4 source-lockdown binding vlan 100.
For 4100i AOS-CX 10.17.1000, what does the no IPv6 source-binding <VLAN-ID> command do?

The no IPv6 source-binding <VLAN-ID> command adds static IPv6 client source binding information to the switch IPv6 binding database. Although DHCPv6 snooping is often used to dynamically populate the binding database, this command is available for manually adding entries to the switch IPv6 binding database. NOTE Statically configured IPv6 binding information supersedes any dynamically collected binding information for the same client. The no form of this command removes the specified binding that was statically configured with the IPv6 source-binding command. The no form has no effect on bindings that were dynamically configured with DHCPv6 snooping. Specifies the ID of an existing VLAN on which the client is conn ected. Range: 1 to 4094.
For 4100i AOS-CX 10.17.1000, what does the IPv6 source-lockdown hardware retry <VLAN-ID> command do?

The IPv6 source-lockdown hardware retry <VLAN-ID> command retries the IPv6 source lockdown hardware programming for a client identified by VLAN and IPv6 address. Specifies the ID of an existing VLAN on which the client is conn ected. Range: 1 to 4094.
For 4100i AOS-CX 10.17.1000, what does the show IPv6 source-binding command do?
The show IPv6 source-binding command shows all IPv6 static source binding information irrespective of source lockdown configuration. Syntax: show IPv6 source-binding.
For 4100i AOS-CX 10.17.1000, what is the syntax of the show IPv6 source-binding command?
Syntax: show IPv6 source-binding

section: Step 2: Configuring L3 gateway in switch A

messages.content: For 4100i AOS-CX 10.17.1000, what does the guide say about Step 2: Configuring L3 gateway in switch A?

Step 2: Configuring L3 gateway in switch A: L3-GW(config)# vlan 100 L3-GW(config-vlan-100)# exit L3-GW(config)# interface 1/1/25 L3-GW(config-if)# vlan trunk allowed 100 L3-GW(config-if)# no shutdown L3-GW(config-if)# exit L3-GW(config)# interface vlan 100 L3-GW(config-if-vlan)# ip address 192.168.1.1/24 Now the clients will be able to ping the gateway IP; but will not be able to communicate with each other due to the isolation enforced with private VLAN. As expected, the ping from client 1 to client 2 will fail in the above example, although the clients are in the same subnet.

topic: Step 2: Configuring L3 gateway in switch A

messages.content: For 4100i AOS-CX 10.17.1000, what does the arp inspection trust command do?

The arp inspection trust command configures the interface as a trusted. All interfaces are untrusted by default. Public arp inspection trust 249 The no form of this command returns the interface to the default state (untrusted). Syntax: arp inspection trust.
For 4100i AOS-CX 10.17.1000, what is the syntax of the arp inspection trust command?
Syntax: arp inspection trust
For 4100i AOS-CX 10.17.1000, what does the arp ip <IP_ADDR> mac <MAC_ADDR> command do?

The arp ip <IP_ADDR> mac <MAC_ADDR> command specifies a permanent static neighbor entry in the ARP table (for IPv4 neighbors). The no form of this command deletes a permanent static neighbor entry from the ARP table. Public arp ip 250 Specifies the IP address of the neighbor or the virtual IP addres s of the cluster in IP format (x.x.x.x), where x is a decimal num ber from 0 to 255.. Range: 4096 to 131072. Default: 131072. Specifies the MAC address of the neighbor or the multicast M AC address in IANA format (xx: xx: xx: xx: xx: xx), where x is a h exadecimal number from 0 to F. Range: 4096 to 131072. Defaul t: 131072. Syntax: arp ip <IP_ADDR> mac <MAC_ADDR>.
For 4100i AOS-CX 10.17.1000, what is the syntax of the arp ip <IP_ADDR> mac <MAC_ADDR> command?
Syntax: arp ip <IP_ADDR> mac <MAC_ADDR>
For 4100i AOS-CX 10.17.1000, what does the arp process-grat-arp command do?

The arp process-grat-arp command enables the processing of gratuitous ARP packets on the individual port or group of L3 ports together. By default, the gratuitous ARP processing is enabled. When gratuitous ARP (GARP) processing is enabled, a switch that is advertising any changes in its MAC through the GARP will reflect in the neighbor table of the switch. However, the switch will not be able to learn the neighbor through the GARP. This configuration is applicable only on L3 interfaces such as ROPs, subinterfaces, and SVIs. The no form of this command disables the processing of gratuitous ARP packets. Syntax: arp process-grat-arp.
For 4100i AOS-CX 10.17.1000, what is the syntax of the arp process-grat-arp command?
Syntax: arp process-grat-arp
For 4100i AOS-CX 10.17.1000, what does the clear arp command do?

The clear arp command clears IPv4 and IPv6 neighbor entries from the ARP table. If you do not specify any VRF or port parameters, ARP table entries are cleared for the default VRF. Specifies a port on the switch. For example: vlan 2. ip <A. B. C. D>|all] IPv6 <X: X: : X: X>|all vrf (Optional) Include an IP address to clear neighbor entries for th at specific address, or use the all parameter to clear entries for all IP addresses. (Optional) Include an IPv6 address to clear neighbor entries for that specific address, or use the all parameter to clear entries fo r all IPv6 addresses. Clears IPv4 and IPv6 neighbor entries for the specified VRF or for all VRFs. If no VRF is specified he default VRF is cleared. Public clear arp 253 Clear neighbor entries for all VRFs Clear neighbor entries for the specified VRF. Syntax: clear arp.
For 4100i AOS-CX 10.17.1000, what is the syntax of the clear arp command?
Syntax: clear arp
For 4100i AOS-CX 10.17.1000, what does the debug arp-security <LOG-CATEGORY> [severity <LEVEL>] command do?

The debug arp-security <LOG-CATEGORY> [severity <LEVEL>] command enables ARP security debug logs. If <SEVERITY> is omitted, all severities are logged. The no form of this command disables ARP security debug logs. <LOG-CATEGORY> severity <LEVEL> Selects the ARP security debug log category. Available categori es are: • all: Selects all ARP security debug log categories. • config: Selects the ARP security config debug log category. inspection: Selects the ARP security inspection debug log • category. • packet: Selects the ARP security packet debug log categor y. Specifies how to filter the ARP security debug logging by settin g the minimum severity level for which debug logging will be pe rformed. The selected severity level and all severities above (m ore severe) will be included in the logging. Syntax: debug arp-security <LOG-CATEGORY> [severity <LEVEL>].
For 4100i AOS-CX 10.17.1000, what is the syntax of the debug arp-security <LOG-CATEGORY> [severity <LEVEL>] command?
Syntax: debug arp-security <LOG-CATEGORY> [severity <LEVEL>]
For 4100i AOS-CX 10.17.1000, what does the IPv6 neighbor mac command do?

The IPv6 neighbor mac command specifies a permanent static neighbor entry in the ARP table (for IPv6 neighbors). The no form of this command deletes a permanent static neighbor entry from the ARP table. <IPv6-ADDR>> Specifies an IP address in IPv6 format (xxxx: xxxx: xxxx: xxxx: x xxx: xxxx: xxxx: xxxx), where x is a hexadecimal number from 0 to F. Range: 4096 to 131072. Default: 131072. Public IPv6 neighbor mac 259 mac <MAC-ADDR>> Specifies the MAC address of the neighbor (xx: xx: xx: xx: xx: xx), where x is a hexadecimal number from 0 to F. Range: 4096 to 131072. Default: 131072. Syntax: IPv6 neighbor <IPv6-ADDR> mac <MAC-ADDR>.
For 4100i AOS-CX 10.17.1000, what is the syntax of the IPv6 neighbor mac command?
Syntax: IPv6 neighbor <IPv6-ADDR> mac <MAC-ADDR>
For 4100i AOS-CX 10.17.1000, what does the show arp inspection interface [<IFNAME>] [vlan <VLAN-ID>] command do?

The show arp inspection interface [<IFNAME>] [vlan <VLAN-ID>] command shows the current configuration of dynamic ARP inspection on an interface. Specifies the interface. Specifies the VLAN ID. Range: 1 to 4094.
For 4100i AOS-CX 10.17.1000, what does the show arp inspection statistics vlan [<VLAN-ID>] command do?

The show arp inspection statistics vlan [<VLAN-ID>] command shows statistics about forwarded and dropped ARP packets. When <VLAN-ID> is not specified, information is shown for all configured VLANs. Specifies the VLAN ID or range of IDs separated by a dash " - ". Range: 1 to 4094.
For 4100i AOS-CX 10.17.1000, what does the show arp inspection vlan [<VLAN-ID>] command do?

The show arp inspection vlan [<VLAN-ID>] command shows the current configuration of dynamic ARP inspection on a VLAN. When <VLAN-ID> is not specified, information is shown for all configured VLANs. Specifies the VLAN ID or range of IDs separated by a dash " - ". Range: 1 to 4094.
For 4100i AOS-CX 10.17.1000, what does the show spanning-tree detail command do?
The show spanning-tree detail command shows spanning tree detail including CIST and corresponding port information. Syntax: show spanning-tree detail.
For 4100i AOS-CX 10.17.1000, what is the syntax of the show spanning-tree detail command?
Syntax: show spanning-tree detail
For 4100i AOS-CX 10.17.1000, what does the show spanning-tree inconsistent-ports command do?

The show spanning-tree inconsistent-ports command shows ports blocked by STP protection functions such as Root guard, Loop guard, BPDU guard, and RPVST guard in addition to MSTI information. Public show spanning-tree inconsistent-ports 208 Specifies the MSTP instance ID. Range: 1 to 4094. <INSTANCE-ID>. Syntax: show spanning-tree inconsistent-ports [instance <INSTANCE-ID>].
For 4100i AOS-CX 10.17.1000, what is the syntax of the show spanning-tree inconsistent-ports command?
Syntax: show spanning-tree inconsistent-ports [instance <INSTANCE-ID>]
For 4100i AOS-CX 10.17.1000, what does the show spanning-tree mst command do?
The show spanning-tree mst command shows MSTP configuration and status information for each instance. Syntax: show spanning-tree mst.
For 4100i AOS-CX 10.17.1000, what is the syntax of the show spanning-tree mst command?
Syntax: show spanning-tree mst
For 4100i AOS-CX 10.17.1000, what does the show spanning-tree mst <INSTANCE-ID> detail 219 command do?

The show spanning-tree mst <INSTANCE-ID> detail 219 command displays MSTP configurations for the given instance ID with corresponding port details. Specifies the MSTP instance number. Range: 0 to 4094. <INSTANCE-ID>. Syntax: show spanning-tree mst <INSTANCE-ID> detail.
For 4100i AOS-CX 10.17.1000, what is the syntax of the show spanning-tree mst <INSTANCE-ID> detail 219 command?
Syntax: show spanning-tree mst <INSTANCE-ID> detail
For 4100i AOS-CX 10.17.1000, what does the show spanning-tree mst interface command do?

The show spanning-tree mst interface command shows MSTP configurations for the given instance ID with corresponding port details. Specifies the MSTP instance number. Range: 0 to 4094. <INSTANCE-ID> <IFNAME>. Syntax: show spanning-tree mst <INSTANCE-ID> interface <IFNAME>.
For 4100i AOS-CX 10.17.1000, what is the syntax of the show spanning-tree mst interface command?
Syntax: show spanning-tree mst <INSTANCE-ID> interface <IFNAME>
For 4100i AOS-CX 10.17.1000, what does the show spanning-tree summary port command do?
The show spanning-tree summary port command shows spanning tree port summary information. Syntax: show spanning-tree summary port.
For 4100i AOS-CX 10.17.1000, what is the syntax of the show spanning-tree summary port command?
Syntax: show spanning-tree summary port
For 4100i AOS-CX 10.17.1000, what does the show spanning-tree summary root command do?

The show spanning-tree summary root command shows spanning tree root summary information. Enables the spanning tree protocol on the switch. The no form of this command disables the spanning tree protocol on the switch. Syntax: show spanning-tree summary root.
For 4100i AOS-CX 10.17.1000, what is the syntax of the show spanning-tree summary root command?
Syntax: show spanning-tree summary root

Syntax: clear spanning-tree statistics [VLAN-ID]

The show capacities rpvst command shows the capacities of RPVST VLANs configurable on a system and RPVST VPORTs supported in a system. Syntax: show capacities rpvst.
For 4100i AOS-CX 10.17.1000, what is the syntax of the show capacities rpvst command?
Syntax: show capacities rpvst
For 4100i AOS-CX 10.17.1000, what does the show capacities-status rpvst command do?
The show capacities-status rpvst command shows the number of RPVST VLANs and RPVST VPORTs currently configured. Syntax: show capacities-status rpvst.
For 4100i AOS-CX 10.17.1000, what is the syntax of the show capacities-status rpvst command?
Syntax: show capacities-status rpvst
For 4100i AOS-CX 10.17.1000, what does the show spanning-tree vlan command do?

The show spanning-tree vlan command displays the spanning tree mode and information on the RPVST instance of the specified VLAN. Specifies the number of a VLAN. <VLAN-ID>. Syntax: show spanning-tree vlan <VLAN-ID>.
For 4100i AOS-CX 10.17.1000, what is the syntax of the show spanning-tree vlan command?
Syntax: show spanning-tree vlan <VLAN-ID>
For 4100i AOS-CX 10.17.1000, what does the show spanning-tree vlan detail command do?

The show spanning-tree vlan detail command displays the spanning tree mode and information on the RPVST instance of the specified VLAN and optionally displays details on the RPVST instance for the VLAN. Specifies the number of a VLAN. <VLAN-ID> Enables and configures the auto re-enable timeout in seconds for all interfaces with BPDU guard enabled. When an interface is disabled after receiving an unauthorized BPDU it will automatically be re-enabled after the timeout expires. The default is for the interface to stay disabled until manually re-enabled. The no form of the command disables BPDU guard timeout. This is the default. Specifies the re-enable timeout in seconds. Range: 1 to 6553 5. <INTERVAL> Configures use of extended system ID. When enabled, the VLAN ID is included in spanning tree packets. Syntax: show spanning-tree vlan <VLAN-ID> detail.
For 4100i AOS-CX 10.17.1000, what is the syntax of the show spanning-tree vlan detail command?
Syntax: show spanning-tree vlan <VLAN-ID> detail

Syntax: diag-dump private-vlan basic

The show capacities private-vlan command shows the maximum number of primary and secondary VLANs per domain and secondary ports per LC that can be configured. Syntax: show capacities private-vlan.
For 4100i AOS-CX 10.17.1000, what is the syntax of the show capacities private-vlan command?
Syntax: show capacities private-vlan

The show capacities-status private-vlan command shows the number of primary VLANs currently configured and the maximum capacity of primary VLANs on the switch. Syntax: show capacities-status private-vlan.

Syntax: show capacities-status private-vlan
For 4100i AOS-CX 10.17.1000, what does the interface lag command do?

The interface lag command creates a Link Aggregation Group (LAG) interface represented by an ID. The no form of this command deletes a LAG interface represented by an ID. Specifies a LAG interface ID. <ID>. Syntax: interface lag <ID>.
For 4100i AOS-CX 10.17.1000, what is the syntax of the interface lag command?
Syntax: interface lag <ID>
For 4100i AOS-CX 10.17.1000, what does the interface persona lag command do?

The interface persona lag command creates a persona LAG, a template for LAG (Link Aggregation Group) interfaces. A persona LAG defines configuration options that can be applied to multiple LAGs for consistency and simplified management. Only LAG interfaces can reference persona LAG templates. The persona LAG name cannot use reserved interface names or formats, such as vlan, lag, or loopback. Configurations defined in a persona LAG are valid only for LAG interfaces. Public interface persona lag 52 The no form of this command deletes the persona LAG interface. Specifies the name of the persona LAG. <NAME>. Syntax: interface persona lag <NAME>.
For 4100i AOS-CX 10.17.1000, what is the syntax of the interface persona lag command?
Syntax: interface persona lag <NAME>
For 4100i AOS-CX 10.17.1000, what is the syntax of the ip address command?
Syntax: ip address <IPv4-ADDR>/<MASK> [secondary]

The show interface persona command shows all interface personas and their attached interfaces. Syntax: show interface persona.

Syntax: show interface persona
For 4100i AOS-CX 10.17.1000, what does the show lacp interfaces command do?

The show lacp interfaces command displays an LACP configuration of the physical interfaces, including VSX s. If an interface name is passed as argument, it only displays an LACP configuration of a specified interface. Optional: Specifies an interface name. <IFNAME> vsx-peer Shows the output from the VSX peer switch. If the switches do not have the VSX configuration or the ISL is down, the output f rom the VSX peer switch is not displayed. This parameter is avai lable on switches that support VSX. Public show lacp interfaces 76. Syntax: show lacp interfaces [<IFNAME>] [vsx-peer].
For 4100i AOS-CX 10.17.1000, what is the syntax of the show lacp interfaces command?
Syntax: show lacp interfaces [<IFNAME>] [vsx-peer]
For 4100i AOS-CX 10.17.1000, what does the vlan trunk native command do?

The vlan trunk native command assigns a native VLAN ID to a LAG interface. The no form of this command removes a native VLAN from a LAG interface and assigns VLAN ID 1 as its native VLAN. <VLAN-ID> Maximum number of VLANs supported: 512 (4100i) Specifies the number of the VLAN ID to assign. The VLAN ID m ust exist. Syntax: vlan trunk native <VLAN-ID>.
For 4100i AOS-CX 10.17.1000, what is the syntax of the vlan trunk native command?
Syntax: vlan trunk native <VLAN-ID>
For 4100i AOS-CX 10.17.1000, what does the diag-dump smartlink basic command do?

The diag-dump smartlink basic command dumps the Smartlink configuration, state and statistics. Configures primary port for a Smartlink group. The no form of this command removes primary port from a Smartlink group. Specifies interface for primary port. <INTERFACE-NAME> Configures secondary port for a Smartlink group. The no form of this command removes secondary port from a Smartlink group. Specifies interface for secondary port. <INTERFACE-NAME> Configures control VLAN in a Smartlink group. The no form of this command removes control VLAN from a Smartlink group. Specifies VLAN ID for a Smartlink group. Public control-vlan 101 Specifies VLANs protected by a Smartlink group. The no form of this command removes VLANs protected by a Smartlink group. Specifies list of VLAN IDs. Range is 1 to 4094. Syntax: diag-dump smartlink basic.
For 4100i AOS-CX 10.17.1000, what is the syntax of the diag-dump smartlink basic command?
Syntax: diag-dump smartlink basic
For 4100i AOS-CX 10.17.1000, what does the show capacities smartlink command do?
The show capacities smartlink command shows Smartlink capacities or Smartlink capacities and status. Syntax: show capacities smartlink | show capacities-status smartlink.

syntax: show capacities smartlink | show capacities-status smartlink

Syntax: show capacities smartlink | show capacities-status smartlink

The show capacities ufd command command show capacities ufd shows UFD session capacity. Command show capacities-status ufd shows UFD session capacity and the number of UFD sessions configured. Syntax: show capacities ufd.
