State information: Disabled by VSX: Public show interface 66 Link state: down for 3 days (since Tue Mar 16 05: 20: 47 UTC 2021) Link transitions: 0 Description: Hardware: Ethernet, MAC address: 04: 09: 73: 62: 90: e7 MTU 1500 Type SFP+DAC3 Full-duplex qos trust none Speed 0 Mb/s Auto-negotiation is off Flow-control: off Error-control: off.
For 4100i AOS-CX 10.17.1000, what does the clear mirror command do?

The clear mirror command clears the mirror statistics for all configured mirror sessions or a specified session Specifies all configured sessions. Specifies a numeric identifier for the session. Range: 1 to 4. Syntax: clear mirror [all | <SESSION-ID>].
For 4100i AOS-CX 10.17.1000, what is the syntax of the clear mirror command?
Syntax: clear mirror [all | <SESSION-ID>]
For 4100i AOS-CX 10.17.1000, what does the destination tunnel <TUNNEL-IPv4-ADDR> source <SOURCE-IPv4-ADDR> command do?

The destination tunnel <TUNNEL-IPv4-ADDR> source <SOURCE-IPv4-ADDR> command specifies the tunnel where all mirrored traffic for the session is transmitted. Only one tunnel destination is allowed per session. You may configure multiple mirror sessions with the same source/destination IP address pair, however, only one of those sessions sharing the same source/destination IP address pair can be enabled at a given time. ERSPAN is not supported leaving the switch by the OOB port. If VRF management is configured for an ERSPAN session, the session will be in "mirror_err_tunnel_oob_port_not_supported" operation status. ERSPAN is not supported leaving the switch encapsulated within another tunnel (e.g. GRE IPv4). When the path to the destination IP address will leave via a tunnel, the session will be in "tunnel_route_resolution_not_populated" operation status. Syntax: destination tunnel <TUNNEL-IPv4-ADDR> source <SOURCE-IPv4-ADDR>.
For 4100i AOS-CX 10.17.1000, what is the syntax of the destination tunnel <TUNNEL-IPv4-ADDR> source <SOURCE-IPv4-ADDR> command?
Syntax: destination tunnel <TUNNEL-IPv4-ADDR> source <SOURCE-IPv4-ADDR>
For 4100i AOS-CX 10.17.1000, what does the mirror session <SESSION-ID> command do?

The mirror session <SESSION-ID> command creates a mirroring session configuration context or enters an existing mirroring session configuration context. From this context, you can enter commands to configure and enable or disable the mirroring session. The no form of this command removes an existing mirroring session from the configuration. Specifies the session identifier. Range: 1 to 4 <SESSION-ID>. Syntax: mirror session <SESSION-ID>.
For 4100i AOS-CX 10.17.1000, what is the syntax of the mirror session <SESSION-ID> command?
Syntax: mirror session <SESSION-ID>
For 4100i AOS-CX 10.17.1000, what is the syntax of the source interface {<PORT-NUM> | <LAG-NAME>} [<DIRECTION>] command?
Syntax: source interface {<PORT-NUM> | <LAG-NAME>} [<DIRECTION>]
For 4100i AOS-CX 10.17.1000, what does the lldp med poe [priority-override] command do?

The lldp med poe [priority-override] command enables MED TLV list in LLDP to advertise for Power over Ethernet Data Link Layer Classification. Also enables the lldp-MED TLV priority to override user configured port priority for Power over Ethernet. When both dot3 and MED are enabled, dot 3 will take precedence. MED TLV is by default enabled for PoE. Priority over-ride is by default disabled. The no form of this command disables MED TLV list in LLDP. [priority-override] System defined name of the interface. Syntax: lldp med poe [priority-override].
For 4100i AOS-CX 10.17.1000, what is the syntax of the lldp med poe [priority-override] command?
Syntax: lldp med poe [priority-override]
For 4100i AOS-CX 10.17.1000, what does the power-over-ethernet command do?

The power-over-ethernet command enables per-interface power distribution. Per-port power is enabled by default with priority low. PoE cannot be disabled for individual ports when Quick PoE is enabled for the entire switch or line module. The no form of this command disables per-interface power distribution. Syntax: power-over-ethernet.
For 4100i AOS-CX 10.17.1000, what is the syntax of the power-over-ethernet command?
Syntax: power-over-ethernet
For 4100i AOS-CX 10.17.1000, what does the power-over-ethernet allocate-by {usage | class} command do?

The power-over-ethernet allocate-by {usage | class} command configures the power allocation method. Power allocation method is initially based on usage. PSE Allocated power value will change to LLDP negotiated power if and when LLDP exchange takes place between PSE and PD. When there is no LLDP negotiation, PSE Allocated Power Value will be the actual instantaneous power draw and reserve power based on actual consumption. In allocate-by class, power allocation is based on PD requested class and PSE allocated power value will be the LLDP negotiated power when LLDP exchange takes place between PSE and PD. When there is no LLDP negotiation, PSE Allocate Power will be based on PD class. Reserve power is based on PD Class. By default, power allocation is by usage. Syntax: power-over-ethernet allocate-by {usage | class}.
For 4100i AOS-CX 10.17.1000, what is the syntax of the power-over-ethernet allocate-by {usage | class} command?
Syntax: power-over-ethernet allocate-by {usage | class}
For 4100i AOS-CX 10.17.1000, what does the power-over-ethernet pre-std-detect command do?

The power-over-ethernet pre-std-detect command before IEEE 802.3 released the first Power over Ethernet standard (802.3af), vendors had shipped PoE capable switches and PD's. HPE Aruba Networking switches are backward compatible and will support both IEEE standard and pre-standard 802.3af Power over Ethernet PD's concurrently. This CLI allows the user to enable or disable pre-802.3af-standard device detection and powering on the specific port. When pre-std-detect is enabled, power will be delivered on PairA only. Default is disabled. The no form of this command resets the action to default. Syntax: power-over-ethernet pre-std-detect.
For 4100i AOS-CX 10.17.1000, what is the syntax of the power-over-ethernet pre-std-detect command?
Syntax: power-over-ethernet pre-std-detect
For 4100i AOS-CX 10.17.1000, what does the power-over-ethernet priority {critical | high | low} command do?

The power-over-ethernet priority {critical | high | low} command sets PoE priority for an interface Specifying critical, high, or low indicates the priority of the interface in the event of power over-subscription. Within the same priority level, higher power-priority line-module ports have higher precedence. With same PoE priority and same line-module priority, lower numbered line-module ports have higher precedence. Per-interface PoE priority is low by default. The no form of this command resets the priority to default PoE priority "low". Syntax: power-over-ethernet priority {critical | high | low}.
For 4100i AOS-CX 10.17.1000, what is the syntax of the power-over-ethernet priority {critical | high | low} command?
Syntax: power-over-ethernet priority {critical | high | low}
For 4100i AOS-CX 10.17.1000, what does the power-over-ethernet threshold <PERCENTAGE> command do?

The power-over-ethernet threshold <PERCENTAGE> command sets the threshold at which the system will send an excess power consumption notification trap. Default value is 80 percentage. The no form of this command resets the action to default. Excess power consumption trap threshold. Range 1-99. <PERCENTAGE>. Syntax: power-over-ethernet threshold <PERCENTAGE>.
For 4100i AOS-CX 10.17.1000, what is the syntax of the power-over-ethernet threshold <PERCENTAGE> command?
Syntax: power-over-ethernet threshold <PERCENTAGE>
For 4100i AOS-CX 10.17.1000, what does the power-over-ethernet trap command do?

The power-over-ethernet trap command this command enables/disables the SNMP trap generation for PoE related events at system level. PoE trap generation is enabled by default. The no form of this command resets the priority to default PoE priority "low". Syntax: power-over-ethernet trap.
For 4100i AOS-CX 10.17.1000, what is the syntax of the power-over-ethernet trap command?
Syntax: power-over-ethernet trap
For 4100i AOS-CX 10.17.1000, what monitoring behavior is documented for Interface and VLAN management?

Interface and VLAN management: Initiates an SSH connection from Aruba AirWave to AOS-CX so that the device outputs from the AOS-CX CLI can be displayed in the Aruba AirWave user inter face. Firmware versions Displays neighbor devices connected to AOS-CX switches Device topology Public Supported features with AirWave and the AOS-CX swi... 124 S u p p o r t e d f e a t u r e s w i t h A i r W a v e a n d t h e A O S - C X s w i...

section: Configuring the AOS-CX switch to be monitored by AirWave

messages.content: For 4100i AOS-CX 10.17.1000, how do you configure the AOS-CX switch to be monitored by AirWave?

To configure the AOS-CX switch to be monitored by AirWave, follow the documented procedure: 1. Enable SNMP on the switch by entering the snmp-server vrf command 2. Configure the SNMP v2C community to public by entering the snmp-server community publ 3. The community-string is used by SNMP v1 and SNMP v2C for unencrypted authentication. SNMP v3 lets.

task: Configuring the AOS-CX switch to be monitored by AirWave

messages.content: For 4100i AOS-CX 10.17.1000, what does the clear accounting-logs command do?

The clear accounting-logs command use this command to clear accounting logs. Once issued, only logs generated after this command is run will be displayed in the output of the show accounting log commands. NOTE This command will not clear logs when the logging accounting-format-native feature is configured. To clear accounting logs on switches with this feature enabled, users should first revert the native accounting format back to the default AOS-CX format by executing the no logging accounting-format-native command. Syntax: clear accounting-logs.
For 4100i AOS-CX 10.17.1000, what is the syntax of the clear accounting-logs command?
Syntax: clear accounting-logs
For 4100i AOS-CX 10.17.1000, what does the logging command do?

The logging command enables syslog forwarding to a remote syslog server. The no form of this command disables syslog forwarding to a remote syslog server. Starting with AOS-CX 10.11, payload information is present in accounting logs. The maximum REST payload that can be sent to RADIUS/TACACS server is 1024 characters, and the maximum of REST payload that can be sent to syslog server is 3500 characters. If this limit is is reached, the log will display three dots (...) to indicate that the log an exceeded the character limit and is incomplete. Public logging 130 {<IPv4-ADDR> | <IPv6-ADDR> | <HOSTNAME>} Selects the IPv4 address, IPv6 address, or host name of the rem ote syslog server. Required. Syntax: logging {<IPv4-ADDR> | <IPv6-ADDR> | <FQDN | HOSTNAME>} {udp [<PORT-NUM>]}.
For 4100i AOS-CX 10.17.1000, what is the syntax of the logging command?
Syntax: logging {<IPv4-ADDR> | <IPv6-ADDR> | <FQDN | HOSTNAME>} {udp [<PORT-NUM>]}
For 4100i AOS-CX 10.17.1000, what does the logging accounting-format-native command do?

The logging accounting-format-native command change the accounting log message format to native Linux format. (Default: AOS-CX format) The 'no' form of this command will change the accounting log message format to AOS-CX format. Syntax: logging accounting-format-native.
For 4100i AOS-CX 10.17.1000, what is the syntax of the logging accounting-format-native command?
Syntax: logging accounting-format-native
For 4100i AOS-CX 10.17.1000, what does the rmon alarm {enable | disable} {index <INDEX> | all} command do?

The rmon alarm {enable | disable} {index <INDEX> | all} command enables and disables the RMON alarm and its index. RMON alarm is enabled by default. Enables the RMON alarm index Disables the RMON alarm index. Public rmon alarm {enable | disable} {index | all} 149 Specifies the RMON alarm index. Range: 1 to 20. all. Syntax: rmon alarm {enable | disable} {index <INDEX> | all}.
For 4100i AOS-CX 10.17.1000, what is the syntax of the rmon alarm {enable | disable} {index <INDEX> | all} command?
Syntax: rmon alarm {enable | disable} {index <INDEX> | all}
For 4100i AOS-CX 10.17.1000, what does the show mac-notify port command do?

The show mac-notify port command displays the MAC notification configuration on a range of ports. [port <PORTS>] Specifies a port, range of ports, or list of ports. Syntax: show mac-notify [port <PORTS>].
For 4100i AOS-CX 10.17.1000, what is the syntax of the show mac-notify port command?
Syntax: show mac-notify [port <PORTS>]
For 4100i AOS-CX 10.17.1000, what does the show snmp agent-port command do?
The show snmp agent-port command displays SNMP agent UDP port number. Syntax: show snmp agent-port.
For 4100i AOS-CX 10.17.1000, what is the syntax of the show snmp agent-port command?
Syntax: show snmp agent-port
For 4100i AOS-CX 10.17.1000, what does the show snmp community command do?
The show snmp community command displays a list of all configured SNMP v1/v2c communities. Syntax: show snmp community.
For 4100i AOS-CX 10.17.1000, what is the syntax of the show snmp community command?
Syntax: show snmp community
For 4100i AOS-CX 10.17.1000, what does the show snmpv3 context command do?
The show snmpv3 context command displays all configured SNMP contexts. Syntax: show snmpv3 context.
For 4100i AOS-CX 10.17.1000, what is the syntax of the show snmpv3 context command?
Syntax: show snmpv3 context
For 4100i AOS-CX 10.17.1000, what does the show snmpv3 engine-id command do?

The show snmpv3 engine-id command displays the configured SNMP v3 snmp engine-id. If the SNMP v3 engine-id is not configured, by default a unique engine-id is created by the switch using a combination of the enterprise OID value and the switch's MAC address. Syntax: show snmpv3 engine-id.
For 4100i AOS-CX 10.17.1000, what is the syntax of the show snmpv3 engine-id command?
Syntax: show snmpv3 engine-id
For 4100i AOS-CX 10.17.1000, what does the show snmpv3 security-level command do?
The show snmpv3 security-level command displays the configured SNMP v3 security level. Syntax: show snmpv3 security-level.
For 4100i AOS-CX 10.17.1000, what is the syntax of the show snmpv3 security-level command?
Syntax: show snmpv3 security-level
For 4100i AOS-CX 10.17.1000, what does the show snmp system command do?
The show snmp system command displays SNMP description, location, and contact information. Syntax: show snmp system.
For 4100i AOS-CX 10.17.1000, what is the syntax of the show snmp system command?
Syntax: show snmp system
For 4100i AOS-CX 10.17.1000, what does the show snmp trap command do?
The show snmp trap command displays all configured SNMP traps/informs receivers. Syntax: show snmp trap.
For 4100i AOS-CX 10.17.1000, what is the syntax of the show snmp trap command?
Syntax: show snmp trap
For 4100i AOS-CX 10.17.1000, what does the show snmpv3 users command do?
The show snmpv3 users command displays all configured SNMP v3 users. For more details on the user enabled status, see snmpv3 security-level. Syntax: show snmpv3 users.
For 4100i AOS-CX 10.17.1000, what is the syntax of the show snmpv3 users command?
Syntax: show snmpv3 users
For 4100i AOS-CX 10.17.1000, what does the show snmp views command do?

The show snmp views command displays the list of all the configured SNMP views. pending_validation Default value that indicates SNMP view is yet to be validated. operational OID and mask validated. Invalid OID/mask. Validation failed for reasons other than OID/mask. invalid failed. Syntax: show snmp views.
For 4100i AOS-CX 10.17.1000, what is the syntax of the show snmp views command?
Syntax: show snmp views
For 4100i AOS-CX 10.17.1000, what does the show snmp vrf command do?
The show snmp vrf command displays the VRF on which the SNMP agent service is running. Public show snmp vrf 166. Syntax: show snmp vrf.
For 4100i AOS-CX 10.17.1000, what is the syntax of the show snmp vrf command?
Syntax: show snmp vrf
For 4100i AOS-CX 10.17.1000, what does the snmpv3 context <NAME> vrf <VRF-NAME> [community <STRING>] command do?

The snmpv3 context <NAME> vrf <VRF-NAME> [community <STRING>] command creates an SNMP v3 context on the specified VRF. The no form of this command removes the specified SNMP context. Specifies the name of the context. Range: 1 to 32 printable ASC II characters, excluding space and question mark (?). Public snmpv3 context 167 vrf <VRF-NAME> Specifies the VRF associated with the context. Default: default. community <STRING> Specifies the SNMP community string associated with the con text. Range: 1 to 32 printable ASCII characters, excluding space and question mark. Default: public. Syntax: snmpv3 context <NAME> vrf <VRF-NAME> [community <STRING>].
For 4100i AOS-CX 10.17.1000, what is the syntax of the snmpv3 context <NAME> vrf <VRF-NAME> [community <STRING>] command?
Syntax: snmpv3 context <NAME> vrf <VRF-NAME> [community <STRING>]
For 4100i AOS-CX 10.17.1000, what does the snmpv3 engine-id <ENGINE-ID> command do?

The snmpv3 engine-id <ENGINE-ID> command configures the SNMP v3 SNMP engine-id allowing an administrator to configure a unique SNMP engine-id for the switch. This engine-id is used by the NMS management tool to identify and distinguish multiple switches on the same network. The no form of this command restores the default engine-id, created by the switch using a combination of the enterprise OID value and the switch's MAC address. SNMP v3 SNMP engine-id in colon separated hexadecimal n otation. <ENGINE-ID>. Syntax: snmpv3 engine-id <ENGINE-ID>.
For 4100i AOS-CX 10.17.1000, what is the syntax of the snmpv3 engine-id <ENGINE-ID> command?
Syntax: snmpv3 engine-id <ENGINE-ID>
For 4100i AOS-CX 10.17.1000, what does the snmpv3 security-level {auth | auth-privacy} command do?

The snmpv3 security-level {auth | auth-privacy} command configures the SNMP v3 security level. The security level determines which SMNPv3 users defined by the command snmpv3 user are able to connect. The no form of this command changes the security level as follows: • no snmpv3 security-level auth: Sets the security level to auth-privacy. • no snmpv3 security-level auth-privacy: Sets the security level to no authentication or privacy, allowing any SNMP user to connect. SNMP v3 users that support authentication, or authentication a nd privacy are allowed. Only SNMP v3 users with both authentication and privacy are al lowed. This is the highest level of SNMP v3 security. Default. Syntax: snmpv3 security-level {auth | auth-privacy}.
For 4100i AOS-CX 10.17.1000, what is the syntax of the snmpv3 security-level {auth | auth-privacy} command?
Syntax: snmpv3 security-level {auth | auth-privacy}
For 4100i AOS-CX 10.17.1000, what does the snmp-server agent-port command do?

The snmp-server agent-port command sets the UDP port number that the SNMP master agent uses to communicate. UDP port 161 is the default port. The no form of this command sets the SNMP master agent port to the default value. Specifies the UDP port number that the SNMP master agent wil l use. Range: 1 to 65535. Default: 161. <PORT>. Syntax: snmp-server agent-port <PORT>.
For 4100i AOS-CX 10.17.1000, what is the syntax of the snmp-server agent-port command?
Syntax: snmp-server agent-port <PORT>
For 4100i AOS-CX 10.17.1000, what does the snmp-server community command do?

The snmp-server community command adds an SNMP v1/SNMP v2c community string. A community string is like a password that controls read/ write access to the SNMP agent. A network management program must supply this name when attempting Public snmp-server community 173 to get SNMP information from the switch. A maximum of 10 community strings are supported. Once you create your own community string, the default community string (public) is deleted. The no form of this command removes the specified SNMP v1/SNMP v2c community string. When no community string exists, a default community string with the value public is automatically defined. Specifies the SNMP v1/SNMP v2c community string. Range: 1 to 32 printable ASCII characters, excluding space and question ma rk. Syntax: snmp-server community <STRING>.
For 4100i AOS-CX 10.17.1000, what is the syntax of the snmp-server community command?
Syntax: snmp-server community <STRING>
For 4100i AOS-CX 10.17.1000, what does the snmp-server historical-counters-monitor command do?

The snmp-server historical-counters-monitor command enables the Remote Network Monitoring agent (rmond) to start collecting historical interface statistics. The no form of this command stops the historical interface statistics collection. Public snmp-server historical-counters-monitor 176. Syntax: snmp-server historical-counters-monitor.
For 4100i AOS-CX 10.17.1000, what is the syntax of the snmp-server historical-counters-monitor command?
Syntax: snmp-server historical-counters-monitor

section: mvrp Sends Multiple VLAN Registration Protocol (MVRP)

messages.content: For 4100i AOS-CX 10.17.1000, what monitoring behavior is documented for mvrp Sends Multiple VLAN Registration Protocol (MVRP)?

mvrp Sends Multiple VLAN Registration Protocol (MVRP): notifications. ospf Sends Open Shortest Path First (OSPFv2) notifications. ospfv3 Sends Open Shortest Path First version 3 (OSPFv3) notifications. port-security Sends Port Security notifications. power Sends Power notifications. power-ethernet Sends Power over Ethernet (PoE) notifications. rmon Sends Remote Network Monitoring (RMON) notifications. rpvst Sends Rapid Per VLANspanning Tree (RPVST) notifications.

topic: mvrp Sends Multiple VLAN Registration Protocol (MVRP)

messages.content: For 4100i AOS-CX 10.17.1000, what does the snmp-server system-contact command do?

The snmp-server system-contact command sets SNMP contact information. The no form of this command removes the SNMP contact information. Specifies SNMP contact information. Range: 1 to 128 printable ASCII characters, except for question mark (?). <INFO>. Syntax: snmp-server system-contact <INFO>.
For 4100i AOS-CX 10.17.1000, what is the syntax of the snmp-server system-contact command?
Syntax: snmp-server system-contact <INFO>
For 4100i AOS-CX 10.17.1000, what does the snmp-server system-location command do?

The snmp-server system-location command sets the SNMP location information. The no form of this command removes the SNMP location information. Specifies the SNMP location information. Range: 1 to 128 prin table ASCII characters, except for the question mark (?). <INFO>. Syntax: snmp-server system-location <INFO>.
For 4100i AOS-CX 10.17.1000, what is the syntax of the snmp-server system-location command?
Syntax: snmp-server system-location <INFO>
For 4100i AOS-CX 10.17.1000, what does the snmp-server vrf command do?

The snmp-server vrf command configures a VRF on which the SNMP agent listens for incoming requests. By default, the SNMP agent does not listen on any VRF. 4100i, 6000, and 6100 only support default VRF. The SNMP agent can listen on multiple VRFs. The no form of this command stops the SNMP agent from listening for incoming requests on the specified VRF. Specifies the name of a VRF. <VRF-NAME>. Syntax: snmp-server vrf <VRF-NAME>.
For 4100i AOS-CX 10.17.1000, what is the syntax of the snmp-server vrf command?
Syntax: snmp-server vrf <VRF-NAME>
For 4100i AOS-CX 10.17.1000, what is the syntax of the snmp-server trap command?
Syntax: snmp-server trap {cpu-utilization | memory-utilization | rmon-events}
For 4100i AOS-CX 10.17.1000, what does the IPv6 mld snooping apply access-list command do?

The IPv6 mld snooping apply access-list command configures the ACL on a particular interface to filter the MLD join or leave packets based on rules set in the particular ACL name. The no form of this command disables the rules set for the ACL. NOTE This configuration will override the ACL associated with IGMP snooping on the corresponding L2 VLAN. Associates an ACL with the IGMP. Public IPv6 mld snooping apply access-list 19 Specifies the name of the ACL. <ACL-NAME> NOTE If the access list is configured for both L2 VLA N and L3 VLAN, the L3 VLAN configuration will be applied. Syntax: IPv6 mld snooping apply access-list <ACL-NAME>.
For 4100i AOS-CX 10.17.1000, what is the syntax of the IPv6 mld snooping apply access-list command?
Syntax: IPv6 mld snooping apply access-list <ACL-NAME>
For 4100i AOS-CX 10.17.1000, what does the no IPv6 mld snooping [auto vlan <VLAN-LIST>] command do?
The no IPv6 mld snooping [auto vlan <VLAN-LIST>] command this command configures the given ports in auto mode, which is the default port mode. The no form of this command disables auto ports.
For 4100i AOS-CX 10.17.1000, what does the no IPv6 mld snooping [blocked vlan <VLAN-LIST>] command do?

The no IPv6 mld snooping [blocked vlan <VLAN-LIST>] command by default ports are configured in auto mode. This command configures the given ports in blocked mode. The no form of this command removes blocked ports. Public IPv6 mld snooping blocked vlan 23.
For 4100i AOS-CX 10.17.1000, what does the no IPv6 mld snooping [fastleave vlan <VLAN-LIST>] command do?

The no IPv6 mld snooping [fastleave vlan <VLAN-LIST>] command configures the specified ports as fastleave ports. Enables the switch to immediately remove an interface from the bridge table upon receiving the leave group message. The no form of this command disables fastleave configuration on the ports.
For 4100i AOS-CX 10.17.1000, what does the no IPv6 mld snooping [forced-fastleave <VLAN-LIST>] command do?

The no IPv6 mld snooping [forced-fastleave <VLAN-LIST>] command configures the given ports in forced fastleave mode. The no form of this command disables forced fastleave configuration on the ports. Public IPv6 mld snooping forced fastleave vlan 28.
For 4100i AOS-CX 10.17.1000, what does the no IPv6 mld snooping [forward vlan <VLAN-LIST>] command do?

The no IPv6 mld snooping [forward vlan <VLAN-LIST>] command by default ports are configured in auto mode. This command configures the given ports in forward mode. The no form of this command disables forward ports.

section: MLD configuration commands for interface VLAN

messages.content: For 4100i AOS-CX 10.17.1000, what does the guide say about MLD configuration commands for interface VLAN?

MLD configuration commands for interface VLAN: Select a command from the list in the left navigation menu. Subtopics IPv6 mld IPv6 mld apply access-list no IPv6 mld IPv6 mld querier IPv6 mld querier interval IPv6 mld querier-wait-time IPv6 mld last-member-query-interval IPv6 mld querier query-max-response-time IPv6 mld robustness IPv6 mld static-group IPv6 mld version IPv6 mld version strict IPv6 mld Public.

topic: MLD configuration commands for interface VLAN

messages.content: For 4100i AOS-CX 10.17.1000, what does the IPv6 mld {enable | disable} command do?

The IPv6 mld {enable | disable} command this command enables or disables MLD on the interface VLAN. The no form of this command disables MLD on the interface VLAN. Required: Enable MLD on the interface VLAN. Required: Disable MLD on the interface VLAN. switch(config)# interface vlan 2 switch(config-if-vlan)# IPv6 mld enable switch(config-if-vlan)# IPv6 mld disable. Syntax: IPv6 mld {enable | disable}.
For 4100i AOS-CX 10.17.1000, what is the syntax of the IPv6 mld {enable | disable} command?
Syntax: IPv6 mld {enable | disable}
For 4100i AOS-CX 10.17.1000, what does the no IPv6 mld command do?
The no IPv6 mld command this command removes all MLD configurations on the interface. Syntax: no IPv6 mld.
For 4100i AOS-CX 10.17.1000, what is the syntax of the no IPv6 mld command?
Syntax: no IPv6 mld
For 4100i AOS-CX 10.17.1000, what does the IPv6 mld querier command do?
The IPv6 mld querier command this command configures MLD querier. The no form of this command disables MLD querier. Syntax: IPv6 mld querier.
For 4100i AOS-CX 10.17.1000, what is the syntax of the IPv6 mld querier command?
Syntax: IPv6 mld querier
For 4100i AOS-CX 10.17.1000, what does the IPv6 mld querier interval command do?

The IPv6 mld querier interval command this command configures MLD querier interval. The default interval-value is 125. Required: 5-300, configures MLD querier interval. NOTE Default interval-value is 125. Use the no ipv 6 mld querier interval command to set interval -value to the default. Syntax: IPv6 mld querier [interval <interval-value>].
For 4100i AOS-CX 10.17.1000, what is the syntax of the IPv6 mld querier interval command?
Syntax: IPv6 mld querier [interval <interval-value>]
For 4100i AOS-CX 10.17.1000, what does the IPv6 mld querier-wait-time command do?

The IPv6 mld querier-wait-time command configures initial MLD querier-wait-time value in seconds. The no form of this command sets the MLD querier-wait-time to the default value of 260 seconds. Note that the wait timer can be configured to any numbers within the 1-300 second range. Configures MLD querier-wait-time to desired value. <QUERIER-WAIT-TIME-VALUE>. Syntax: IPv6 mld querier-wait-time <QUERIER-WAIT-TIME>.
For 4100i AOS-CX 10.17.1000, what is the syntax of the IPv6 mld querier-wait-time command?
Syntax: IPv6 mld querier-wait-time <QUERIER-WAIT-TIME>
For 4100i AOS-CX 10.17.1000, what does the IPv6 mld last-member-query-interval command do?

The IPv6 mld last-member-query-interval command this command configures MLD last member query interval value in seconds. The default interval-value is 1 second. Required: 1-2, configures MLD last-member-query-inter val. NOTE Default interval-value is 1 second. Use the no IPv6 mld last-member -query-interval command to set interval-value to the default. Public IPv6 mld last-member-query-interval 49. Syntax: IPv6 mld last-member-query-interval <interval-value>.
For 4100i AOS-CX 10.17.1000, what is the syntax of the IPv6 mld last-member-query-interval command?
Syntax: IPv6 mld last-member-query-interval <interval-value>
For 4100i AOS-CX 10.17.1000, what does the IPv6 mld querier query-max-response-time command do?

The IPv6 mld querier query-max-response-time command this command configures MLD max response time value in seconds. The default max-response-time-value is 10 seconds. max-response-time-value Required: 10-128, configures MLD querier max-response -time. NOTE Default max-response-time-value is 10 sec onds. Use the no IPv6 mld querier query-ma Public IPv6 mld querier query-max-response-time 50 x-response-time command to set max-res ponse-time-value to the default. Syntax: IPv6 mld querier query-max-response-time <response-time>.
For 4100i AOS-CX 10.17.1000, what is the syntax of the IPv6 mld querier query-max-response-time command?
Syntax: IPv6 mld querier query-max-response-time <response-time>
For 4100i AOS-CX 10.17.1000, what does the IPv6 mld robustness command do?

The IPv6 mld robustness command this command configures MLD robustness. The robustness value represents the number of times the querier retries queries on the connected subnets. The default robustness-value is 2 seconds. Public IPv6 mld robustness 51 Required: 1-7, configures MLD robustness. <VALUE>. Syntax: IPv6 mld robustness <VALUE>.
For 4100i AOS-CX 10.17.1000, what is the syntax of the IPv6 mld robustness command?
Syntax: IPv6 mld robustness <VALUE>

messages.content: For 4100i AOS-CX 10.17.1000, what does the guide say about Internet Group Management Protocol (IGMP)?

Internet Group Management Protocol (IGMP): In a network where IP multicast traffic is transmitted for various multimedia applications, you can use the switch to reduce unnecessary bandwidth usage on a per-port basis by configuring IGMP (Internet Group Management Protocol). IGMPv3 (RFC 3376) and IGMPv2 (RFC 2236) are the current RFCs for IGMP. In the factory default state (IGMP disabled), the switch simply floods all IP multicast traffic it receives on a given VLAN through all ports on that VLAN (except the port on which it received the traffic). This can result in significant and unnecessary bandwidth usage in networks where IP multicast traffic is a factor. Enabling IGMP allows the ports to detect IGMP queries and report packets and manage IP multicast traffic through the switch. IGMP is useful in multimedia applications such as LAN TV, desktop conferencing, and collaborative computing, where there is Multi Point communication; that is, communication from one to many hosts, or communication originating from many hosts and destined for many other hosts.
For 4100i AOS-CX 10.17.1000, what does the ip igmp apply access-list command do?

The ip igmp apply access-list command configures the ACL on a particular interface to filter the IGMP join or leave packets based on rules set in the particular ACL name. Public ip igmp apply access-list 84 The no form of this command unconfigures the rules set for the ACL. NOTE This configuration will override the ACL associated with IGMP snooping on the corresponding L2 VLAN. Associates an ACL with the IGMP. Specifies the name of the ACL. Syntax: ip igmp apply access-list <ACL-NAME>.

The ip igmp last-member-query-interval command configures an IGMP last member query interval value in seconds on an interface, depending on the command context you are in. The no form of this command sets the value to a default of 1 second on an interface. Specifies an IGMP last-member-query-interval on the inter face. Default: 1 second. Range: 1-2 seconds. <INTERVAL-VALUE>. Syntax: ip igmp last-member-query-interval <INTERVAL-VALUE>.

Note that the wait timer can be configured to any numbers within the 1-300 second range. Configures IGMP querier-wait-time to desired value. <QUERIER-WAIT-TIME>. Syntax: ip igmp querier-wait-time <QUERIER-WAIT-TIME>.

Default: 10 seconds. Range: 10-128 seconds. <RESPONSE-TIME> Public ip igmp querier query-max-response-time 91. Syntax: ip igmp querier query-max-response-time <RESPONSE-TIME>.

For 4100i AOS-CX 10.17.1000, what does the ip igmp router-alert-check command do?

The ip igmp router-alert-check command enables or disables IGMP router alert check for IGMP packets. IGMP packets without the router alert field set are dropped if router alert check is enabled. Router alert check is disabled by default. The no form of this command disables router alert check for IGMP packets. Enable IGMP router alert check. Disable IGMP router alert check. Enabling IGMP router alert check on interface VLAN 2: switch(config)# interface vlan 2 switch(config-if-vlan)# ip igmp router-alert-check enable Disabling IGMP router alert check on interface VLAN 2: switch(config)# interface vlan 2 switch(config-if-vlan)# ip igmp router-alert-check disable switch(config)# interface vlan 2 switch(config-if-vlan)# no ip igmp router-alert-check enable. Syntax: ip igmp router-alert-check [enable | disable].

For 4100i AOS-CX 10.17.1000, what does the ip igmp snooping (config mode) command do?

The ip igmp snooping (config mode) command configures drop-unknown and fastlearn modes on the ports. While IGMP snooping is enabled, the traffic will be forwarded only to ports that made an IGMP request for the multicast. Drop unknown filters ensure that packets are not forwarded to ports that did not make a request for the traffic stream. This could either be a filter across all VLANs (vlan-shared) or per VLAN (vlan-exclusive). The default is vlan-shared. Fast learn enables the port to learn group information when receiving a topology change notification. By default, fast learn is not enabled on ports. Drop unknown filters ensure that packets are not forwarded to ports that did not make a request for the traffic stream. Enables a shared VLAN filter on the switch. Default is vlan-sh ared. Syntax: ip igmp snooping.

Syntax: ip igmp snooping

For 4100i AOS-CX 10.17.1000, what does the troubleshoot multicast [igmp|mld|pim|pim6] [basic|config|advance] command do?

The troubleshoot multicast [igmp|mld|pim|pim6] [basic|config|advance] command troubleshoot multicast networks while focusing on specific multicast settings. This targeted approach to troubleshooting helps identify and fix issues in large, complex networks that include multiple virtual routing domains (VRFs) and interfaces. AOS-CX troubleshooting works with both IPv4 and IPv6 multicast protocols, and can address multicast group membership problems with IGMP and MLD, and verify the routing paths for multicast traffic using PIM or PIM6. igmp mld basic config advance parameters "<query><word>" Troubleshoot IGMP multicast traffic. Troubleshoot MLD multicast traffic. Display basic information for multicast errors on the network, in cluding the error message, message ID, severity level, and when the message was last reported. Syntax: troubleshoot multicast [igmp|mld|pim|pim6] [basic|config|advance].

messages.content: For 4100i AOS-CX 10.17.1000, what does the apply qos threshold-profile command do?

The apply qos threshold-profile command applies a threshold profile globally to all Ethernet and LAG interfaces on the switch, or to a specific interface. When applied globally, the specified threshold profile is configured only on Ethernet interfaces and LAGs that do not already have their own schedule profile. The same profile can be applied both globally and locally to an interface. This guarantees that an interface always uses the specified threshold profile, even if the global profile is changed. The no form of this command removes the specified threshold profile from an interface, and causes it to use the global threshold profile. This is the only way to remove a threshold profile override from an interface. A profile can only be deleted once it is no longer applied to any interface. Syntax: apply qos threshold-profile <THRESHOLD-NAME>.

messages.content: For 4100i AOS-CX 10.17.1000, what does the qos queue-profile command do?

The qos queue-profile command creates a new QoS queue profile and switches to the config-queue context for the profile. Or, if the specified QoS queue profile exists, this command switches to the config-queue context for the profile.. A queue profile maps queues to CoS values. Each profile has two, four, or eight queues numbered 0 to 7. The larger the queue number, the higher its priority during transmission scheduling. Public qos queue-profile 46 The no form of this command removes the specified QoS queue profile. Only profiles that are not currently applied can be removed. Specifies the name of the QoS queue profile to create or config ure. Range: 1 to 64 alphanumeric characters, including period (.), underscore (_), and hyphen (-). <NAME>. Syntax: qos queue-profile <NAME>.

document_title: Quality Of Service Guide (4100I, 6000, 6100 Switch Series)

messages.content: For 4100i AOS-CX 10.17.1000, what does the qos schedule-profile command do?

The qos schedule-profile command creates a QoS schedule profile and switches to the config-schedule context for the profile. If the specified schedule profile exists, this command switches to the config-schedule context for the profile. The schedule profile determines the order in which queues are selected to transmit a packet, and the amount of service defined for each queue. Specifies the name of the QoS queue profile to create or config ure. Range: 1 to 64 alphanumeric characters, including period (.), underscore (_), and hyphen (-). <NAME>. Syntax: qos schedule-profile <NAME>.

messages.content: For 4100i AOS-CX 10.17.1000, what does the qos threshold-profile command do?

The qos threshold-profile command creates a QoS threshold profile and switches to the config-threshold context for the profile. If the specified threshold profile exists, this command switches to the config-threshold context for the existing profile. The threshold profile determines the action to take when a threshold is exceeded for each queue. A threshold profile is composed of up to. Each queue defines the action to take when buffer utilization exceeds a specific threshold. Configure queues with the command queue. The no form of this command removes the specified QoS threshold profile. Only profiles that are not currently applied to an interface can be removed. NOTE In 4100i Switch Series, only one profile can be created. Syntax: qos threshold-profile <NAME>.

For 4100i AOS-CX 10.17.1000, what does the https-server authentication password command do?

The https-server authentication password command enables authentication using username and password, which corresponds to the default authentication mechanism. Enabling the password authentication mode disables the certificate authentication mode. NOTE Only one authentication method can be enabled at a time. Syntax: https-server authentication password.

For 4100i AOS-CX 10.17.1000, what does the https-server max-user-sessions command do?

The https-server max-user-sessions command sets the maximum amount of concurrent open sessions for any given user through the HTTPS server. The amount of concurrent open sessions may have an impact on system performance, so it is recommended to set this value to the minimum necessary. Specifies the maximum number of user sessions allowed. Defaul t: 6. Maximum value: 8. <SESSION-AMT>. Syntax: https-server max-user-sessions <SESSION-AMT>.

For 4100i AOS-CX 10.17.1000, what does the https-server rest access-mode command do?

The https-server rest access-mode command changes the REST API access mode. The default mode is read-write. This command does not affect Central connections, which have permission to alter configurations regardless of the access mode set on the switch. Selects the read/write mode. Allows POST, PUT, PATCH, and DE LETE methods to be called on all configurable elements in the s witch database. Selects the read-only mode. Write access to most switch resou rces through the REST API is disabled. Setting the mode to read-write on the REST API allows POST, PUT, PATCH, and DELETE methods to be called on all configurable elements in the switch database. By default, REST APIs in the device are in the read-write mode. Some switch resources allow POST, PUT, PATCH, and DELETE regardless of REST API mode. Syntax: https-server rest access-mode {read-only | read-write}.
For 4100i AOS-CX 10.17.1000, what is the syntax of the https-server rest access-mode command?
Syntax: https-server rest access-mode {read-only | read-write}

messages.content: For 4100i AOS-CX 10.17.1000, what does the guide say about VLAN interfaces?

VLAN interfaces: Hardware (system) interfaces • Hardware interfaces are of type system. • Hardware interfaces are included in the database automatically. • Interfaces of type system cannot be added or deleted. LAG interfaces • LAG interfaces are of type lag. • You can use the DELETE method to delete a LAG interface. Example of creating a LAG interface with member ports 1/1/1 and 1/1/2: Method and URI: POST "/rest/v10.xx/system/interfaces" Request body: Public Considerations for ports and interfaces 60 { "name": "lag50", "vrf": "/rest/v10.xx/system/vrfs/default", "type": "lag", "interfaces": [ "/rest/v10.xx/system/interfaces/1%2F1%2F1", "/rest/v10.xx/system/interfaces/1%2F1%2F2" ] }.
For 4100i AOS-CX 10.17.1000, what does the guide say about Behavior?

Behavior: It can be used in a collection, a specific resource, and in a request including wildcards. If the request for any resource includes an attribute that does not exist, a bad request (HTTP 400) will be returned and the request will fail. There is no limit on the number of attributes that can be specified in the request. Use case It allows for more efficient data retrieval and can reduce the amount of data transferred over the network. It reduces CPU and memory consumption, especially in cases with resources that contain many attributes and high amount of data, which also improves the response time to the user. Examples.
For 4100i AOS-CX 10.17.1000, what does the guide say about Valid VLAN collection attribute list?

Valid VLAN collection attribute list: GET https://<IP>/rest/<version>/system/vlans/1? attributes=name, type, admin { "admin": "up", "name": "DEFAULT_VLAN_1", "type": "default" } Public Attributes parameter 64 Count parameter Syntax The count parameter indicates the request wants to obtain the total number of entries related to a resource. A successful response contains a JSON format with the count key and a number as the value. It works with requests including wildcard and other query parameters. Omitting it in the request is equivalent to specifying count=false. Including it in the request as count= is equivalent to specifying count=true. A request with count to a specific resource is valid and will return 1. Use Cases The user gets the number of entries instead of the values of entries. Requests to dynamic or protected resources are not limited. Limitations It is not supported for notifications. Examples Single resource A request with a count to a specific resource is valid and will return 1.

For 4100i AOS-CX 10.17.1000, what does the guide say about CLI Management Interface?

CLI Management Interface: Introduction Firmware Site Distribution (FWSD) is an efficient feature designed to allow the client to upgrade multiple devices in a network simultaneously. The orchestrator coordinates the entire upgrade process, providing better control and monitoring. The feature can handle a large number of devices, making it suitable for networks of various sizes. Devices in the network can retrieve firmware images from seed devices, minimizing the load on external sources. This feature can be used by a CLI command or a REST request. It's possible to execute the firmware site distribution via CLI and the REST management interface request at the same time. The switch can handle up to 2 concurrent requests if the requests share the same SSL session. Although it is possible, it is recommended to use only one to avoid any inconvenience. FWSD is particularly suitable for environments with a large number of devices which are locally co-located. FWSD operates as follows: 1. The orchestrator determines which devices require upgrades. 2. The orchestrator sends upgrade commands to the pending devices. 3.

VSX peer switches and REST API access: If Virtual Switching Extension (VSX) is enabled, you can access the REST API of a peer switch without having to separately log into or manage a session cookie from that peer switch. To access a peer REST API from your connected switch, insert /vsx-peer in the URI path after the server URL and before the REST API and version identifier. For example: Public.

topic: VSX peer switches and REST API access

messages.content: For 4100i AOS-CX 10.17.1000, what does the guide say about Configuration maximums?

Configuration maximums: • Maximum number of subscribers per switch: 50 • Maximum number of topics in one subscription message: 2000 Public Authorization 165 Enabling the notifications subsystem on a switch The AOS-CX real-time notifications subsystem relies on the REST API, so the REST API must be enabled on the switch and VRF from which you want to receive notifications. HTTPS server must be enabled on the specified VRF. The VRF you specify determines from which network the HTTPS server can be accessed. You can enable access on multiple VRFs, including user-defined VRFs. Establishing a secure Web Socket connection through a web browser Prerequisites • Access to the switch REST API must be enabled. The REST API access mode can be either read-only or read/write. • The web browser you use must support the secure Web Socket Protocol. Procedure 1. Open a web browser page and log in to the switch Web UI or the REST API. The session cookie is managed by the browser and is shared among browser tabs. 2.

messages.content: For 4100i AOS-CX 10.17.1000, what does the clear dot1x authenticator statistics interfac command do?

The clear dot1x authenticator statistics interfac command clears the 802.1X authentication statistics associated with the port and all the authenticator clients attached to this port. If no interface is specified, the statistics is cleared for all 802.1X enabled ports. Specifies the interface name. <IF-NAME>. Syntax: clear dot1x authenticator statistics [interface <IF-NAME>].

For 4100i AOS-CX 10.17.1000, what does the aaa authentication port-access mac-auth command do?

The aaa authentication port-access mac-auth command enables or disables MAC authentication globally or at the port-level. Public Port access MAC authentication commands 502 Configures the MAC address format that the switch must use in the RADIUS request message. The no form of the command resets the MAC address format to the default, no-delimiter. Syntax: aaa authentication port-access mac-auth {enable | disable}.
For 4100i AOS-CX 10.17.1000, what is the syntax of the aaa authentication port-access mac-auth command?
Syntax: aaa authentication port-access mac-auth {enable | disable}
For 4100i AOS-CX 10.17.1000, what does the aaa authentication port-access mac-auth auth command do?

The aaa authentication port-access mac-auth auth command configures the RADIUS authentication method for MAC authentication. Following are the MAC authentication methods supported: • CHAP • PAP NOTE The PEAP-MSCHAPv2 method of authentication is not supported. The no form of the command resets the authentication method to the default, chap. Syntax: aaa authentication port-access mac-auth auth-method {chap | pap}.

syntax: aaa authentication port-access mac-auth auth-method {chap | pap}

messages.content: For 4100i AOS-CX 10.17.1000, what is the syntax of the aaa authentication port-access mac-auth auth command?
Syntax: aaa authentication port-access mac-auth auth-method {chap | pap}

syntax: aaa authentication port-access mac-auth auth-method {chap | pap}

messages.content: For 4100i AOS-CX 10.17.1000, what does the aaa authentication port-access mac-auth cache command do?

The aaa authentication port-access mac-auth cache command enables cached reauthentication on a port. Cached reauthentication allows MAC reauthentications to succeed when the RADIUS server is unavailable. Users who are already authenticated, retain their currently assigned RADIUS attributes. The no form of the command disables cached reauthentication. Syntax: aaa authentication port-access mac-auth cached-reauth.
For 4100i AOS-CX 10.17.1000, what is the syntax of the aaa authentication port-access mac-auth cache command?
Syntax: aaa authentication port-access mac-auth cached-reauth
For 4100i AOS-CX 10.17.1000, what does the aaa authentication port-access mac-auth quiet command do?

The aaa authentication port-access mac-auth quiet command configures the period during which the switch does not try to authenticate a rejected client. The no form of the command resets the quiet period to the default, 60 seconds. Specifies the quiet period (in seconds). Default: 60. Range: 0 to 65535. Public aaa authentication port-access mac-auth quiet-peri... 509 a a a a u t h e n t i c a t i o n p o r t - a c c e s s m a c - a u t h q u i e t - p e r i. Syntax: aaa authentication port-access mac-auth quiet-period <PERIOD>.
For 4100i AOS-CX 10.17.1000, what is the syntax of the aaa authentication port-access mac-auth quiet command?
Syntax: aaa authentication port-access mac-auth quiet-period <PERIOD>
For 4100i AOS-CX 10.17.1000, what does the aaa authentication port-access mac-auth radiu command do?

The aaa authentication port-access mac-auth radiu command configures the MAC authentication server group globally or for a particular port. The no form of the command resets the authentication server group to the default value, radius. When configured on a port, the no form of the command resets the server group on that port to the globally configured group. If no global RADIUS server group is configured, the no form of the command resets the configuration to the default group, radius. NOTE When the RADIUS server group for MAC authentication is updated on a port, any existing clients on the port that were authenticated using the previous globally configured group will associate with the new group for the port during the next re-authentication cycle. Syntax: aaa authentication port-access mac-auth radius server-group <GROUP-NAME>.

syntax: aaa authentication port-access mac-auth radius server-group <GROUP-NAME>

messages.content: For 4100i AOS-CX 10.17.1000, what is the syntax of the aaa authentication port-access mac-auth radiu command?
Syntax: aaa authentication port-access mac-auth radius server-group <GROUP-NAME>

messages.content: For 4100i AOS-CX 10.17.1000, what does the aaa authentication port-access mac-auth reaut command do?

The aaa authentication port-access mac-auth reaut command enables periodic MAC reauthentication of authenticated clients on the port. The no form of the command disables periodic MAC reauthentication on the port. Syntax: aaa authentication port-access mac-auth reauth.
For 4100i AOS-CX 10.17.1000, what is the syntax of the aaa authentication port-access mac-auth reaut command?
Syntax: aaa authentication port-access mac-auth reauth
For 4100i AOS-CX 10.17.1000, what does the aaa authentication port-access client-limit m command do?

The aaa authentication port-access client-limit m command configures the data client limit on the multidomain enabled interface. By default, the data client limit on a multidomain enabled interface is 1, and the maximum number of data clients supported on a multidomain enabled port is 5. Specifies the maximum data client limit on the multidomain ena bled interface. Range: 1 to 5. <DATA-CLIENT-LIMIT> Enables the system to detect and prevent loops using LLDP neighbor information. The feature operates during the vulnerability window before traditional loop prevention protocols become active. This feature is disabled by default. The no form of the command removes the configuration. Syntax: aaa authentication port-access client-limit multi-domain <DATA-CLIENT-LIMIT>.
For 4100i AOS-CX 10.17.1000, what is the syntax of the aaa authentication port-access client-limit m command?
Syntax: aaa authentication port-access client-limit multi-domain <DATA-CLIENT-LIMIT>

The show port-access port-security violation clie command shows information on the number of client-limit-exceeded security violations that have occurred. The output can be filtered by interface. Specifies all interfaces. Specifies the interface name or a comma-separated list of inte rfaces, or a hyphen-separated interface range. Syntax: show port-access port-security violation client-limit-exceeded interface.

For 4100i AOS-CX 10.17.1000, what does the clear dot1x supplicant statistics command do?

The clear dot1x supplicant statistics command clears the 802.1X supplicant statistics associated with the interface. If no interface is specified, the statistics are cleared for all 802.1X supplicant-enabled interfaces. Public clear dot1x supplicant statistics 672 Specifies the range of VLAN interfaces for which the supplicant statistics are cleared. <IFRANGE> Configures the time period (in seconds) to wait for a potential 802.1X authenticator on the other end before considering the link to be non-802.1X-capable and opening the interface on the data-plane. On a timeout, the switch will not use the authentication result to determine the forwarding behavior of the interface until a link flap. If not set, the switch will wait for the 802.1X authentication cycle to complete before determining the forwarding state of the interface. Syntax: clear dot1x supplicant statistics [interface <IFRANGE>].
For 4100i AOS-CX 10.17.1000, what is the syntax of the clear dot1x supplicant statistics command?
Syntax: clear dot1x supplicant statistics [interface <IFRANGE>]
For 4100i AOS-CX 10.17.1000, what does the policy (supplicant) command do?

The policy (supplicant) command creates an 802.1X supplicant policy on the system. The no form of the command deletes the 802.1X supplicant policy on the system. Specifies the name of the policy. (Maximum 32 characters). <POLICY-NAME>. Syntax: policy <POLICY-NAME>.

Syntax: policy <POLICY-NAME>

messages.content: For 4100i AOS-CX 10.17.1000, what does the aaa radius-attribute group command do?

The aaa radius-attribute group command configures an existing RADIUS server group for which the configured RADIUS attributes will be included in request packets. Enters the config-radius-attr context. The no form of this command unconfigures the RADIUS server group for the configured RADIUS attributes. NOTE Nas-id and tunnel-private-group-id attributes only apply to port access requests. Nas-ip-addr attributes only apply to management user requests. Specifies an existing RADIUS server group name. <GROUP-NAME>. Syntax: aaa radius-attribute group <GROUP-NAME>.

messages.content: For 4100i AOS-CX 10.17.1000, what does the mac-notify traps {aged | learned | moved | removed} command do?

The mac-notify traps {aged | learned | moved | removed} command configures a Layer 2 or VXLAN interface to generate SNMP trap notifications for up to four different types of MAC address related events on the trunk or access in physical or lag interfaces. MAC notification trap addition to or removal from an interface can be in any combination, quantity, or order. The addition of existing configured traps or removal of non-configured traps will be accepted and ignored. NOTE The mac-notify feature must be enabled globally for any interface configurations to generate SNMP traps. Enabling mac-notify traps may impact the system performance on networks with a large number of mac-notify events. The no form of this command removes the traps from the interface. Notifies when a MAC address aged out on the interface. Syntax: mac-notify traps {aged | learned | moved | removed}.
For 4100i AOS-CX 10.17.1000, what is the syntax of the mac-notify traps {aged | learned | moved | removed} command?
Syntax: mac-notify traps {aged | learned | moved | removed}

messages.content: For 4100i AOS-CX 10.17.1000, what does the guide say about mvrp Sends Multiple VLAN Registration Protocol (MVRP)?

mvrp Sends Multiple VLAN Registration Protocol (MVRP): notifications. ospf Sends Open Shortest Path First (OSPFv2) notifications. ospfv3 Sends Open Shortest Path First version 3 (OSPFv3) notifications. port-security Sends Port Security notifications. Public snmp-server host 63 power Sends Power notifications. power-ethernet Sends Power over Ethernet (PoE) notifications. rmon Sends Remote Network Monitoring (RMON) notifications. rpvst Sends Rapid Per VLANspanning Tree (RPVST) notifications.

For 4100i AOS-CX 10.17.1000, what does the snmpv3 user <NAME> command do?

The snmpv3 user <NAME> command creates an SNMP v3 user and adds it to an SNMP v3 context. The SNMP v3 security level (set with command snmpv3 security-level) determines which users are allowed to authenticate. The no form of this command removes the specified SNMP v3 user. NOTE When updating the authentication protocols and privacy protocols for the existing SNMP v3 users, you must also update the access level. Otherwise, the access level will be set to read-only. <NAME> access-level auth <AUTH-PROTO> Specifies the SNMP v3 username. Range 1 to 32 printable ASCII characters, excluding space and question mark (?). Syntax: snmpv3 user <NAME>.
For 4100i AOS-CX 10.17.1000, what is the syntax of the snmpv3 user <NAME> command?
Syntax: snmpv3 user <NAME>
